Listings (left) + Leaflet map (right). Click a listing to zoom/highlight the marker; click a marker to jump to the listing.
What you’re seeing: apartments for purchase (source-specific). Sector borders are approximate OSM admin relations. Late-evening busyness is a composite proxy within ~300m: food/drink density + transit density + culture venues + plaza/pedestrian-zone signal + pedestrian-friendly street length.
(filters)
Overlays (100m grid): each square shows OSM POI counts binned into a 100m×100m grid over Chișinău; color intensity is log-scaled density within that grid cell.
Map legend: pin confirmed pin unconfirmed pin mismatch
#1
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€169,950 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, equipped; autonomous heating + A/C).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 96.6 sqm, 3 separate rooms, 2 bathrooms, two enclosed heated balconies. Investor ➖ — strong “rent tomorrow” condition, but family-sized unit + higher ticket can narrow tenant pool. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — convenient daily life + parks/transit nearby, but Ciocana isn’t the city’s most “boutique” vibe.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/spec is compelling (2 baths + warm floors, autonomous heating, big enclosed balconies), but you should pressure-test the building quality/management and the price-per-sqm for this pocket of Ciocana.

Negotiation strategy

Use inspection leverage: ask for proof/age of the autonomous heating system, balcony heating legality/installation quality, and recent building maintenance (elevators, common areas). If anything is dated/DIY, negotiate. Target price range (guess, comps-dependent): ~€160k–€167k; if everything is genuinely high-quality and turnkey, expect less room.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, practical family apartment with rare comfort features (2 bathrooms, warm floors, autonomous heating) and strong everyday infrastructure. You are NOT buying: a central, nightlife-first micro-location; your upside is livability, not “old-town charm.”

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

Listing Summary

  • URL: https://999.md/ro/101568096
  • Title: Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.
  • Price: EUR 169950
  • Size: 96.6 sqm (stated)
  • Rooms: 3
  • Floor: 6/11 (stated in the ad)
  • Building type: Not stated in the ad.
  • Condition: Repaired/renovated (as stated)
  • Key amenities: autonomous heating; air conditioning; 2 bathrooms; 2 enclosed/heated balconies; furnished; appliances; underfloor heating (bathrooms)
  • Location: Ciocana; str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2

Apartment Description

Facts stated in the ad: The ad describes a 3-room apartment in Chișinău (Ciocana) at str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2, with a stated total area of 96.6 sqm. It mentions autonomous heating, air conditioning, that the apartment is repaired and furnished, equipped with appliances, and states 2 bathrooms.

Critical Evaluation of the Apartment

Facts stated in the ad:

  • Layout: 3 rooms; stated as decomandate (separate).
  • Size: 96.6 sqm stated.
  • Bathrooms: 2 stated (with underfloor heating).
  • Comfort: autonomous heating; air conditioning; enclosed/heated balconies (as stated).
  • Condition: repaired/renovated and furnished stated.

Reasonable inferences (verify):

  • Heated/enclosed balconies can add usable space, but verify insulation quality and whether modifications were permitted.
  • Noise exposure likely depends heavily on street vs courtyard orientation; verify on-site (day + evening).

Open questions / risks:

  • Not stated in the ad: monthly maintenance costs, utility bills, elevator condition, building year, legal/doc status, exact inclusions of furniture/appliances.
  • Verify: building management quality, upcoming repairs, and parking terms (if any).

Normalized scoring (inference; rubric v1)

  • Apartment score (0-10): 7.6 (strong size + comfort features; unknown building/elevator/fees)
  • Micro-area score (0-10): 7.4 (claimed amenities + strong OSM density nearby; verify noise)
  • Macro-area score (0-10): 6.9 (Ciocana is practical; lifestyle/prestige depends on micro-location)

Scoring note: scores are a standardized estimate for ranking; they are not facts from the ad and should be validated at viewing / via documents.

Pros

  • Large stated size (96.6 sqm) for a 3-room unit.
  • Autonomous heating + A/C stated.
  • Two bathrooms stated.
  • Two enclosed/heated balconies stated.
  • Repaired and furnished, appliances included (as stated).

Cons

  • Building-level details and running costs are not stated.
  • Noise/orientation details are not stated.

Risks / Unknowns

  • Monthly costs / utilities unknown.
  • Elevator/building maintenance unknown.
  • Exact inclusions (furniture/appliances) not fully specified.
  • Balcony enclosure/heating setup should be verified.

Verdict

  • Resident verdict: Promising on paper as a family-sized apartment, but verify building upkeep, noise, and running costs.
  • Investor verdict: Potentially rentable if the building/location check out; confirm costs and demand vs price.
  • Best for: families wanting space + comfort features; buyers comfortable verifying at viewing.
  • Price positioning: Not confirmable from the ad alone; implied ~EUR 1759/sqm from stated size—comps check is essential.

Micro-area (local guide)

Facts stated in the ad: The ad claims strong nearby infrastructure within ~5 minutes’ walk (kindergarten, school, market, supermarkets including Kaufland, bank) and good public transport access.

Reasonable inferences (verify): If accurate, daily errands should be very convenient; expect typical traffic/noise patterns for a major street.

Risks / questions: Verify actual walk times and do an evening noise check at the building entrance and inside the unit.

Macro-area (local guide)

Facts stated in the ad: Sector/district is Ciocana.

Reasonable inferences (resident viewpoint): Sector labels help, but block-by-block reality matters; prioritize noise, building condition, and commute convenience once the exact micro-location is confirmed.

  • Price: €169,950
  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Ciocana

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: it’s sold as “white variant” (shell/unfinished), so budget time + cash for full fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (105 m², 3 rooms, parks nearby; but 16th floor can be polarizing) Investor ➖ (new build + parking helps; but finish costs + resale depends on building quality/management) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (green area vibe, panoramic glazing, balcony/logia x2)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (new monolithic building, autonomous heating, elevators/security features, green micro-area), but the listing has consistency/verification flags and the apartment is unfinished.

Negotiation strategy

First, verify basics in writing: exact building этажность, your unit floor, total area breakdown, and what “variantă albă” includes (heating, electrical panel, plaster level, screed, radiators, door, glazing spec). Pricing angle: use finish cost + risk buffer. A reasonable starting anchor is ~165–175k (guess), aiming to land ~170–180k depending on documentation, real этажность/floor, and what’s actually included.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room (105 m²) new-build shell in a greener Botanica pocket with park access and modern building amenities. You are NOT buying: a move-in-ready home or a “no-surprises” listing—details (notably floor vs number of floors) need hard confirmation before you emotionally commit.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build, “euro renovation”, fully furnished + full appliance stack; likely only minor personalization.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground; check school/traffic noise before committing)
Investor ➖ (strong “rent-ready” spec, but premium pricing means yields depend heavily on exact building reputation + demand for this street)
Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Centru address, high floor, panoramic glazing, lots of parks nearby)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great turnkey package in a central new-build, but at this price you should push hard on proof of quality (developer/building management) and total monthly carrying costs.

Negotiation strategy

Use “risk discount” leverage: high floor + big windows (heat/glare), new-build finishing quality uncertainty, and agency listing. Ask for documentation (handover/acceptance, utilities, heating service history) and negotiate on friction. Target price range (guess): 205k–215k if everything checks out; if any quality/maintenance red flags appear, treat 219k as a walk-away anchor.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, central, turnkey 3-room + living with strong comfort features (autonomous heat, AC, elevator, security systems).
You are NOT buying: a “quiet, low-risk” building by default—new-builds can hide management/maintenance issues; verify the building ecosystem, not just the apartment.
High floor + panoramic glass can feel premium… or punish you with summer heat and wind noise if execution is mediocre.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances included; modern building services).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + closed courtyard + playground + nearby school/kindergarten is the whole “easy daily life” package. Investor ➖ — strong rentability signals, but the price is high enough that yield may be tight unless you’re targeting premium tenants. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — lots of food/parks nearby, good transit access, and 8/9 floor usually means better light and less street-level noise.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — looks like a polished, comfort-heavy apartment in a lively micro-area, but you should push on price and verify noise/parking/elevator realities before paying “turnkey premium.”

Negotiation strategy

Treat the listing as “priced for convenience.” Your leverage is uncertainty: actual building quality, ongoing fees, parking situation, and boulevard noise. Target price range (guess): 5–10% below ask if the boulevard-facing exposure/parking/HOA costs aren’t clearly favorable; otherwise negotiate on inclusions (furniture, appliances, parking) more than headline price.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, fully-equipped, comfort-first home (autonomous heat + underfloor heating + AC + security/closed yard). You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-activity pocket—this is an amenity-dense zone that can also mean traffic, crowds, and parking friction. If you hate uncertainty, demand: exact orientation (courtyard vs boulevard), proof of sound insulation performance, and a clear parking arrangement.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready for “normal life” (cosmetic condition), but expect a refresh and a careful check of the balconies/loggias and windows.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (kindergarten in the courtyard + school nearby, 4-room flexibility) Investor ➖ (4-room demand can be narrower; works best as family rental if price is right) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (convenient services/parks nearby, but Ciocana is more practical than charming)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional family layout + big “usable area” story via 3 loggias, but the panel building + cosmetic repair means you should not pay “fully renovated” pricing.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (comps unknown; this is a best-effort discount for panel stock + cosmetic condition + uncertainty around sellable area vs loggias). Push on: (1) 87.1 m² “on papers” vs claimed 100+ with loggias, (2) panel building depreciation/utility costs, (3) any wear in bathrooms/kitchen and the open loggia exposure.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-first Ciocana base with lots of light (triple aspect) and outdoor spill space (3 loggias). You are NOT buying: a turnkey designer renovation or a “true 100 m²” legally counted interior area. If you want comfort-per-euro and daily convenience (groceries + parks nearby), it’s compelling—at the right number.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Spre vânzare apartament cu 4 camere sau 3 cu living (salon) în sec. Ciocana bd. Mircea cel Bătrân. Priveliște trilaterală. Starea locativă. Nu are mirosuri. Geamuri termopane pe tot perimetrul. 3 loje, una fiind cea m…”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 11 grocery shops, 14 food/drink places, 36 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~49.5/km², grocery density ~38.9/km², transit-stop density ~21.2/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs patience — new-build in “white box” stage with delivery stated for Dec 2027.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (85 m², 2 bathrooms, playground, secured courtyard, elevator) Investor ➖ (long wait to delivery; resale/rent assumptions depend on final handover + finishes) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (quieter edge-of-core feel; limited nearby food/drink in the immediate 300m)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid size/floorplan and modern-complex features, but you’re paying today for a 2027 promise and finishing costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €84,000–€89,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) 2027 delivery risk buffer, (2) “white box” finish scope clarity, (3) parking price separation (subterranean often priced separately), (4) contract penalties/guarantees for delays, (5) staged payments tied to construction milestones.

Bottom line

You are buying: a mid/high-floor (8/12) new-build 3-room layout (2 bedrooms + living) with 2 bathrooms, autonomous heating, security/video, and a family-oriented complex. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in, a proven building track record yet, or a walk-out-the-door cafe/restaurant scene (based on the provided nearby POI counts).

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€390,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Tudor Strișcă, 8/7
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready, high-spec finish — but it’s “luxury systems” heavy, so future maintenance/servicing is part of the deal.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (space + 2 baths + secured yard/playground; watch the high-floor logistics) Investor: ➖ (premium price point narrows tenant pool; strong only for executive/corporate rental) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (panoramic views, terrace/BBQ access, smart home, park-adjacent)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is impressive (163 m², private lift, garage/terrace), but at this price you need proof of building quality, legal clarity on “private” elements, and predictable monthly costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €350k–€375k (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps for this micro-area/building). Angle: treat the “designer renovation” as partially subjective value; push for a discount unless documentation is airtight. Ask to itemize what’s included: furniture/appliances, smart-home hardware/licensing, garage fit-out, terrace rights, any storage. Make the offer contingent on (1) full technical inspection (HVAC/underfloor heating zones, windows, waterproofing for terrace/BBQ exit), (2) building docs + HOA budget/fees + reserve fund, (3) confirmation of private lift access rules and long-term maintenance responsibility.

Bottom line

You are buying: a penthouse-like lifestyle in a new monolith building — views, privacy, and “I don’t share my corridor” vibes. You are NOT buying: low running costs, simple upkeep, or a super-liquid resale at any price (this is a niche, high-ticket product). If the garage/terrace/private lift are truly clean and transferable, it’s a standout; if any of those are fuzzy, it’s overpriced risk.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — sold “without renovation”, so assume full finish budget + time before it feels like home.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, two loggias; good day-to-day infrastructure) Investor ➖ (area demand is strong, but “no renovation” + unknown building/HOA realities compress yields) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Central sector, lots of food/drink density; convenient city living)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the core is attractive (Centru + 71 m² + mid-floor + elevator + bilateral layout), but “no renovation” is where deals go to die unless you buy it right.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on renovation risk + unknowns (actual condition of plumbing/electrics, windows, loggia waterproofing, elevator/common areas). Push for a price that prices in a full modern finish. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €78,000–€83,000. If the building/common areas are unusually clean and documents are straightforward, stretching toward €84–86k can still be rational.

Bottom line

You are buying a well-sized, classic Central-sector “blank canvas” in a practical 9-storey block format. You are NOT buying turnkey comfort — budget for renovation friction, noise/dust timeline, and possible surprises in an older stock building. If you negotiate hard, this can become a high-utility home in a genuinely convenient micro-area.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — renovated, furnished, and fully equipped, but the “developer selling” angle still warrants paperwork diligence.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (130 m² duplex + 2 bathrooms + storage/wardrobe; good internal separation) Investor ➖ (high ticket; rental demand depends on exact building quality and parking reality) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (duplex feel, top-floor-ish light/air; quieter, more residential vibe)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong apartment spec (duplex, 130 m², 2 baths, floor heating), but location vibes look more residential than lively and the price is premium for Botanica unless the building is truly top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €190,000–€205,000 (I don’t have local comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push on: (1) “open” parking vs guaranteed space, (2) elevator/roof/upper-floor maintenance risk, (3) developer sale terms and any finishing/technical warranties, (4) noise/wind and summer heat load at floor 10.

Bottom line

You are buying a big, modern-feeling duplex apartment that lives more like a small house in the sky. You are NOT buying a restaurant-heavy, late-evening pedestrian neighborhood — it reads greener/quiet with essentials nearby. If the building is well-managed and the finishes are real (not photo-magic), it’s a compelling lifestyle buy at the right price.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: quality renovation + furnished/with appliances + autonomous heating and some “premium comfort” extras.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (93 m², 3-bed + living, quiet courtyard feel) Investor ➖ (great rentability in Center, but 4-floor stock + price may cap yield) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (walkable core, restaurants nearby, “closed courtyard” calm)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong Central location + renovated, but you’re paying a “finished product” premium; make the price prove itself via comps and technical checks.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €190k–€205k (best-effort; exact comps not provided). Ask for: (1) proof/details of the autonomous heating system (age/service), (2) confirmation of parking rights (owned vs assigned vs informal), (3) building/roof condition and any HOA/repair fund plans, (4) actual layout legality for “3 rooms + living.” If any uncertainty (roof, documents, parking), price it in immediately.

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, calm-feeling Center apartment with strong day-to-day walkability and “comfort tech” (AC, floor heating, smart-home). You are NOT buying: a new-build amenity tower or guaranteed high-yield rental; the building category/age risk and resale ceiling matter. You’re paying for: location + turnkey condition — so verify the fundamentals (docs, systems, building health) before you overpay.

€230,000 · 4 rooms · Centru · str. Alexandru Botezatu, 4
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (the listing claims a good renovation + autonomous heating), but verify building/legal status because it reads like a small ground-household setup.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 4 rooms + separated rooms + courtyard/parking/garages is a rare “city + space” combo. Investor ➖ — could rent well due to Centru, but liquidity depends heavily on documents/building type. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — central, walkable, lots of amenities; feels like “private house vibe” without leaving downtown.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the core proposition is strong (Centru + yard + 2 garages), but uncertainty around type of building/ownership structure is the make-or-break.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €205k–€220k (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted offer to price in legal/technical uncertainty and renovation variance). Push on: (1) exact property type (“apartment” vs part of a house / land share), (2) cadastral extract + encumbrances, (3) autonomous heating permits/installation papers, (4) what exactly transfers with the deal (both garages? land/courtyard rights?), (5) moisture/insulation and soundproofing in an older “fond locativ” context. Use the seller’s own “possible bargaining” note to anchor.

Bottom line

You are buying: central walkability with an unusually private feel (yard + parking + garages). You are NOT buying: a standard, easy-to-resell elevator-building apartment unless the documents and building type confirm it. If paperwork is clean, it’s a scarce product; if it’s messy, walk away fast.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Центр города на земле большой двор на 3 семьи два гаража, металлический и котельцовый, 3 минуты от улицы Виеру комнаты раздельные, санузел совмещённый хорлший ремонт, автономка, по цене возможен торг”.

Additional stated detail: “Caracteristici (999.md panels): Balcon/ lojie; Compartimentare; Dezvoltator; Etaj; Euroreparație; Fond locativ; Grup sanitar; Inălțimea tavanelor; Living; Loc de parcare; Număr de camere; Număr de etaje; Starea aparta…”. Also noted: “Центр города на земле большой двор на 3 семьи два гаража, металлический и котельцовый, 3 минуты от улицы Виеру комнаты раздельные, санузел совмещённый хорлший ремонт, автономка, по цене возможен торг”. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Balcon/ lojie, Compartimentare, Dezvoltator, Etaj, Euroreparație, Fond locativ, Grup sanitar, Inălțimea tavanelor, Living, Loc de parcare, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Starea apartamentului, Suprafață bucătărie, Suprafață locativă, Suprafață totală, Tip clădire. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Sector (from listing fields): Centru.

Street (from listing fields): Alexandru Botezatu.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~130.9/km², grocery density ~24.8/km², transit-stop density ~21.2/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

#13
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€245,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · strada Avram Iancu 32
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, equipped; low risk if the “quality repair” claim checks out in person.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 separate bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, courtyard/playground, school next door. Investor ➖ — strong rental location signal, but price is high and “developer/owner” ambiguity adds friction. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — central-ish vibe without tourist chaos; walkable daily life and good transit.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (100 m², 3 beds + living, new brick building, floor 2/8, autonomous heat), but you’re paying a premium and need proof on build/finish quality and legal cleanliness.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €220,000–€235,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have reliable comps here). Anchor with: (1) verify actual net area vs total, (2) elevator/parking terms, (3) noise/privacy at floor 2, (4) HVAC/boiler service history, (5) “direct from owner” vs developer representation. If parking isn’t included or documents are messy, push harder.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, family-friendly, modern 3-bedroom layout with two baths in a secured newer complex in Centru. You are NOT buying: a quiet top-floor lightbox or a bargain; floor-2 urban noise/visibility and premium pricing are the trade-offs. If the finishes and paperwork pass inspection, this is a strong “pay for convenience” home — just don’t overpay by default.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€121,230 · 3 rooms · Poșta Veche · str. Ceucari, 14
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Not move-in ready: new-build in progress, sold as “white variant” (expect finishing costs + timeline risk).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 baths + 2 bedrooms + playground + courtyard) Investor ➖ (works if delivery + finishes stay on budget; area isn’t a tourist/short-stay magnet) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (convenient basics nearby, but not an evening-restaurant hotspot)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional 3-room layout in a new block with parking options, but you’re paying new-build pricing while taking construction/hand-over and fit-out risk.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a written delivery timeline, penalties/escrow terms, and a full spec sheet (windows, heating, elevator brand, sound insulation). Push for incentives instead of headline discount: included underground parking, discounted finishing package, or staged payments tied to milestones. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €112k–€118k if no parking included / €118k–€125k if underground parking + clear handover terms are included.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, family-friendly new-build shell with strong day-to-day convenience (groceries/transit) and a sensible 2nd-floor plan. You are NOT buying: guaranteed near-term move-in, a fully finished home, or a vibrant central nightlife micro-location. You’re also buying developer execution risk—make the contract do the heavy lifting.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€139,900 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · str. Petru Zadnipru
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, with appliances + AC; expect mostly “taste/finishing” tweaks, not structural work.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, balcony, playground/park nearby reads very family-forward. Investor ➖ — rentable size and transit are good, but panel-block + mid-periphery micro-area can cap upside unless bought right. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — daily convenience is fine; nightlife/cafe density is modest (more practical than charming).

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout (90 m², 6/9, 2 bathrooms) and “done” condition, but pricing needs to justify panel building + Ciocana location.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€134,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have local comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Press on: panel building risk discount, renovation age/quality proof (wiring/plumbing), elevator/common-area condition, and true parking reality (assigned vs “available”). Ask for: exact year of renovation, utility bills (winter), and documentation for “separate entrance” claim.

Bottom line

You are buying space + practicality: a big, family-sensible 3-room with a living room and 2 bathrooms in a services-heavy area. You are NOT buying architectural charm or a prime-central vibe; resale will track micro-location and building condition more than finishes. If the renovation is genuinely recent and systems are updated, this can be a very comfortable “set-and-forget” home — at the right price.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready, but “cosmetic repair” in a Khrushchevka usually means you’ll eventually want a modernization pass (kitchen/bath + electrics).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms on 60 m² can work well if you accept compact storage and smaller bedrooms. Investor ➖ — rentable, but building class + 4/5 walk-up limits premium tenants and resale upside. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — quiet/green is good, but the immediate cafe/restaurant density looks thin.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid “live-now” unit in a practical area, but walk-up + older stock + likely hidden capex should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on building type (Khrushchevka), 4th-floor walk-up, and the likelihood of near-term upgrades despite being livable. Ask directly about roof condition (top floors), stairwell/common areas, piping, and heating reliability. Target price range: €84,000–€90,000 (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted buyer’s range, not a market-validated comp set).

Bottom line

You are buying: a functional 3-room, 60 m², secondary-market home in a calm, serviceable Botanica pocket with parks nearby. You are NOT buying: a “modern building experience” (elevator/amenities) or a high-street, restaurant-heavy micro-location. If you can win the price, it’s a pragmatic home base; if they hold firm, you’ll likely find similar stock elsewhere with better trade-offs.

You are NOT buying a turnkey designer interior or a proven ‘prime’ micro-location — your outcome depends on finishing cost control and paperwork clarity. Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid “easy living” package, but the immediate surroundings look thin on daily amenities; buy only if the exact spot feels connected in real life.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €103,000–€110,000 (comps unknown — using a caution discount for peripheral micro-location + agency listing + 64 m², 3-room layout). Push on: (1) lack of walkable retail/food, (2) any build-quality/snags typical for new blocks, (3) confirm parking is included vs optional/paid, (4) verify autonomous heating docs + monthly building fees.

Bottom line

You are buying a clean, modern, low-friction apartment in a new brick building (elevator, security/video, closed yard). You are NOT buying a walk-out-your-door neighborhood with cafés, groceries, and street life. If the commute lines up and the block is well-run, it’s a comfortable “home base”; otherwise it’ll feel isolated fast.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + 82 m² is a practical layout, but confirm schools/daycare access + noise/parking. Investor ➖ — new-build demand helps, but micro-area retail density looks weak; liquidity depends on final delivery/quality. Lifestyle buyer ❌ — low immediate cafe/grocery vibe within ~300 m suggests you’ll drive/ride for daily life.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — decent “new build 3-room” proposition on paper, but you’re buying developer execution + surrounding convenience risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €86,000–€92,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-discount range). Use developer leverage points: ask for a direct discount OR value add (finished interior package, kitchen/wardrobes credit, parking/storage, notary/transaction costs, better payment schedule). Demand a written spec: exact finishes, insulation/windows, heating system, elevator brand, and delivery timeline with penalties.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern monolithic-structure apartment in a developer complex with “new build” appeal. You are NOT buying a proven building (yet) or a walk-out-the-door neighbourhood convenience cluster. If the delivery/spec is strong and the discount lands, it can be a solid functional home — otherwise it’s a price trap with finishing surprises.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s a new-build developer unit with strong “spec sheet” promises; confirm actual stage/fit-out, handover timeline, and what’s included (finishes vs shell).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + claimed 100 m², greenery nearby, and stated new infrastructure (school/kindergarten) reads family-friendly. Investor ➖ — new-build can rent well, but returns hinge on true net area, completion risk, and competition from similar units in the same complex. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — high ceilings (~2.85 m) + panoramic glazing + “quiet elevator / better sound” pitch; likely comfortable daily living if execution matches claims.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the building package sounds good (monolith structure, ventilated facade, gas boiler, premium lift), but you’re paying developer pricing without seeing the hard details (true layout, net area, view/noise, and delivery/acceptance conditions).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €135,000–€145,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: exact usable vs total area, balcony/loggia count, orientation/view, floorplan efficiency, parking/storage availability and price, contract milestones, penalties for delays, warranty terms, and what “premium” actually includes. If the unit is already finished/ready, your leverage shifts to comparing against similar new-build listings nearby.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern new-build proposition in Botanica with a “comfort + energy efficiency” narrative (ventilated insulated facade, triple glazing, high ceilings, gas boiler). You are NOT buying: a proven resale with known neighbors/maintenance history — developer promises must be verified on-site and in contract. You are also buying: the micro-area’s calm/green bias (lots of nearby parks in OSM signals) more than a restaurant/nightlife hub (low food/drink count within 300m).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — big layout + terrace + playground + secure courtyard; elevator matters on top floor. Investor ➖ — unique penthouse can rent well, but liquidity/resale is more niche and price-per-sqm scrutiny is brutal. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — top-floor privacy, views, terrace, garages, and walk-to-food density nearby.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is strong (146 sqm duplex + terrace + security + elevator), but top-floor/10th-floor risk and “Durlești marketed as Centru” pricing means you should push hard on value and building quality proof.

Negotiation strategy

Price the two closed garages separately—don’t let them be used to justify an inflated apartment price.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, duplex “house-in-the-sky” with terrace and security in a lively, amenity-dense pocket. You are NOT buying: easy resale liquidity or zero-maintenance—roof/terrace and top-floor building systems are where surprises live. If the building checks out, this is a lifestyle win; if not, you’ll overpay for headaches.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €118,000–€125,000 (best-effort). I don’t have verified comps for this street/building, so treat this as a negotiation anchor based on (a) “white box” finish and (b) typical risk/discount for higher floors + completion scope. Ask for: proof of building developer/commissioning status, HOA/maintenance, utility bills estimate for autonomous heating, and whether underground parking is included or extra. If parking is not included, push harder on price.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, security-forward Botanica complex with a view, 2 bathrooms, and real “city convenience” radius. You are NOT buying: a turnkey home — finishing + furnishing is the real project, and the 11th-floor experience lives or dies by elevators, insulation, and wind/noise.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

#25
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€258,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Nicolae Testemițanu
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build, finished to a “Euro renovation” standard, furnished + appliances, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family ➖ (good layout + 2 baths, but 12th floor and potential wind/elevator dependence) Investor ➖ (turnkey helps, but price looks “retail”; yield may be tight unless you nail tenant profile) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (view, modern finish, big open living/kitchen, near major retail + restaurants)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong turnkey product in a solid Central area, but you should treat the price as “top shelf” and try to buy the finish + view without overpaying.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for the exact build year, developer handover docs, monthly building fees, and what’s included (furniture brand list, appliance models, warranty proofs). At 258k for ~75 m² it implies ~€3.4k/m² (high for many Chișinău micro-markets). Without verified comps, my best-effort target is €240k–€250k if everything is as advertised; if furniture/appliances are premium and the building is truly top-tier, you may land closer to €250k–€258k. If any “new renovation” risks show up (smells, uneven finishes, poor soundproofing), push toward €230k–€240k.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, high-floor, turnkey Central apartment with a clean 2-bedroom + open living/kitchen plan. You are NOT buying a quiet low-floor family nest or a bargain-per-square-meter deal. You’re paying for convenience + aesthetics + view; make sure the building quality and running costs justify it.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (quality renovation + double glazing + autonomous heating), but top-floor risk checks matter.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + playground nearby + lots of green space signals, but verify noise/traffic on Alba Iulia. Investor ➖ — rentable area, yet 9/9 liquidity depends on roof/elevator/building condition. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — bright unit, good transit access, Buiucani is generally “liveable-city” territory.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional package at a plausible price, but 9/9 and “series block” quality risk should buy you a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €78,000–€83,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) top-floor/roof liability, (2) any elevator downtime, (3) building management fees/repairs, (4) layout mismatch (listing says “3 bedrooms” yet “3 rooms” headline), (5) documentation for “new construction” claim vs typical series block.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 71 m², 3-room Buiucani home with autonomous heating and decent day-to-day convenience. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/prime-central vibes, nor risk-free top-floor ownership unless roof + elevator + insulation check out. If the building is genuinely solid and the roof is recently redone, this can be a very clean value play at the right number.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely move-in ready (new-build shell/turnkey depends on what “Smart Home” package includes; expect you’ll still budget for finishing/furnishings unless explicitly delivered finished).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + 2 bathrooms + proximity claims to school/kindergarten) Investor ➖ (good new-build liquidity, but 15th floor + area amenity density looks modest) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (modern tower living, but not a “walk-out-to-cafes-and-parks” micro-vibe based on nearby POI counts)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid modern 3-room size in a new complex, but price needs to justify high-floor tower trade-offs and the relatively thin immediate amenity/park signal.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €95,000–€102,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: developer inventory/competition inside the same complex, finish level included/excluded, any delays in commissioning/management fees, and the high-floor risk (wind/noise/elevator dependence). If it’s delivered fully finished with documented smart-home spec + quality materials, your range can drift upward.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, high-floor, new-build 3-room with 2 bathrooms, balcony/terrace, elevator, autonomous heating, and “security-stack” features (intercom/video/alarm) in a developer-led complex. You are NOT buying: a proven, leafy, café-dense pedestrian micro-neighbourhood right outside the door (nearby food/parks signal looks weak). High floor = views/light + less street noise, but also more reliance on building maintenance and elevator uptime.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: building year/constructor, monthly maintenance, heating type, insulation/windows, elevator condition, parking, and proof the furniture/appliances are included. Target price range: €175,000–€185,000 (comps not provided; if the building is truly modern/new-build quality and the interior is excellent, you may land closer to €185k–€190k).

Bottom line

You are buying: a family-sized, turnkey 3-room setup with a sensible layout and two bathrooms. You are NOT buying: a nightlife/tourist micro-area — it’s more leafy and residential than “always busy.” At this price, the building quality + running costs matter as much as the apartment photos.

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 4 grocery shops, 5 food/drink places, 147 parks/playgrounds, and 3 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

€83,900 · 3 rooms · Râșcani · str. Nicolae Dimo, 29/3
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work (sold “as-is” / without renovation; budget and timeline will decide).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (school/kindergarten behind the building + green area nearby is the core win) Investor ➖ (works if you can renovate efficiently; but 55–62 m² “3 rooms” is niche and may cap rent) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (quiet/green, but not a restaurant/transit hotspot based on nearby POI signals)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid micro-location for daily life, but the “no renovation” condition + compact size means you should buy only if the price compensates for renovation risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €74,000–€80,000 (best-effort; comps unknown). Use: (1) no renovation (unknown wiring/plumbing), (2) small 3-room layout risk (resale pool narrower), (3) parking is open (not guaranteed). Ask for: full cadastral extract confirming 55 m², confirmation of heating/utilities status, and any building maintenance/roof/plumbing history for a 5-floor “cotileț” block.

Bottom line

You are buying: a quiet, green-pocket Râșcani base near a park/forest edge with family infrastructure nearby. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort or a “city buzz” address; you’re signing up for renovation decisions and cost variance. If you can execute renovation cleanly, this becomes a very livable 2-bedroom-ish home; if not, it will feel cramped and unfinished fast.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€180,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · strada Ion Nistor
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — renovated (“euroreparație”), functional 3-bed layout, 2 bathrooms; verify build quality + utilities under load (top floor).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 81 m², 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, schools/services nearby; check noise/wind + elevator reliability at floor 13. Investor ➖ — strong central demand and amenities, but price point is high; yields depend on finish/parking and building reputation. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — panoramic views, central walkability (lots of food/parks) and good transit; top-floor vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (Centru + 81 m² + top-floor views + underground parking), but at €180k you should force clarity on building quality, elevators, and real comps.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps not provided): €165k–€175k. Push on: “13/13 risk” (roof/overheating, elevator downtime), agency listing premium, and demand proof (recent comparable sales/rents). Make the underground parking and renovation quality the swing factors: if both are truly strong, pay closer to the top of the range.

Bottom line

You are buying: a central, amenity-dense micro-area with a big-city feel and a top-floor panoramic apartment that’s easy to live in day 1. You are NOT buying: a ‘risk-free’ building — top-floor + elevator dependence means you must diligence maintenance, roof, and service charges. If the building management is solid and the parking is deeded/real, this becomes a clean long-term hold; otherwise, don’t overpay for the view.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — big layout (119 m²), 2 baths, wardrobe, terrace, playground + quieter residential feel. Investor ➖ — good size, but area signals low restaurant density; rental demand depends on who you target (families vs expats). Lifestyle buyer ➖ — terrace helps, but micro-area looks more practical than vibrant (groceries >> cafés).

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong plan + modern complex, but top-floor exposure + 2026 completion + finishing costs mean price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €128k–€138k (best-effort). I don’t have true comps here, so treat this as a risk-adjusted bid: push on 2026 delivery, “white box” finishing budget, and top-floor trade-offs; ask for incentives (parking spot discount, staged payments, or a finishing credit).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room in a modern complex with terrace, 2 bathrooms, autonomous heating, and strong building amenities/security. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in, certainty on handover timing, or a high-café/late-evening street-life micro-location. Watch-outs: top-floor heat/wind/noise exposure and verify elevator reliability + roof/terrace waterproofing details in the developer docs.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: “white-box” finish in a new-build means the hard infrastructure is there (heating, glazing, elevator), but you’re still budgeting time + cash for full interior fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 92 m², 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, balcony, playground nearby; top-half floor can feel quieter. Investor ➖ — rentable once finished, but white-box + 9th floor liquidity depends on building quality/management and actual area demand. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — modern systems (autonomous heat + underfloor), panoramic windows, parking options.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the spec is attractive (new-build, 92 m², 2 baths, underfloor heating), but Poșta Veche is micro-area sensitive and price only makes sense if the building/HOA, sound insulation, and exposure/views check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €145,000–€152,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Use “white-box” cost-to-finish + any unknowns (exact building quality, elevator count/reliability, parking deed/rights, utilities/commissioning, HOA fees) as leverage. Ask for: (1) exact finish scope, (2) acceptance/commissioning docs, (3) parking terms (owned vs allocated), (4) orientation + noise sources, (5) total monthly building costs.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, higher-floor new-build shell with strong comfort features (autonomous + underfloor heating, big windows, security/video, elevator) and a family-friendly layout. You are NOT buying a turnkey home — finishing costs, timelines, and workmanship risk are on you. At this price, the building’s execution (soundproofing, common areas, management) matters more than the feature list.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (good layout + 2 bathrooms, but top floor and finish-work timeline can be a hassle) Investor: ➖ (location is strong, but white-box + premium-ish pricing reduces immediate yield) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (parks + quieter, non-touristy micro-area; good if you value greenery over nightlife)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong Buiucani park-adjacent positioning and new-build fundamentals, but top-floor (13/13) + white-box finish should translate into a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on total cost-to-finish (white variant), top-floor risk (roof/heat/wind exposure), and resale liquidity vs mid-floors. Start with a target range of €145k–€155k (comps not provided, so this is a best-effort discount band rather than a comp-backed number). If they won’t move on price, push for seller-paid finishing allowances / documented warranty / confirmed building management fees.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room new-build (92 m²) in Buiucani with autonomous heating, elevator, and park-heavy surroundings. You are NOT buying: move-in-ready convenience (white variant) or the easiest-to-resell floor level (it’s 13 out of 13).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Buyer fit

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong space/plan for the money, but top-floor risk + micro-area amenity density is modest; you want price protection and clean documentation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €130,000–€138,000 (best-effort guess; no direct comps provided, so treat as a negotiation anchor rather than a valuation).

You are NOT buying: a high-footfall, restaurant-heavy central lifestyle micro-area. If the terrace/roof/elevator/management checks out, this can be a very livable “space-first” buy — otherwise your future headaches will be maintenance, not layout.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (newer building + quality renovation + autonomous/underfloor heating signals “done” rather than “project”).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (2 bedrooms + courtyard/playground + schools/kindergartens nearby) Investor: ➖ (ultra-central helps liquidity, but entry price per m² looks steep unless finishes/building are truly premium) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (right on the main boulevard; walk-to-everything; restaurants/transit density)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location + modern building package, but the price implies “prime” and you need to verify noise, build quality, and actual net area.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on (1) boulevard noise/air + (2) resale ceiling + (3) any ambiguity in the stated area (the text shows 72.2 m², but there’s also a suspicious “722 m²” artifact). Ask for: cadastral extract, exact usable vs total area, heating bills, HOA/maintenance, and what’s included (kitchen, built-ins). Target price range: €300,000–€320,000 (comps not provided, so treat as a negotiating band, not a valuation). If they won’t move: request included furniture/appliances, parking terms (if any), and a fast-close discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a central, high-convenience, newer-building 3-room with modern heating and a “closed courtyard” lifestyle. You are NOT buying: quiet, low-traffic serenity—being on the main boulevard almost always means noise/dust trade-offs. This works best if you value daily walkability and want zero renovation drama more than maximum space-per-euro.

Final verdict

Negotiation strategy

You are buying: a renovated, furnished, top-floor(ish) new-build-style apartment with 2 bathrooms and strong day-to-day convenience. You are NOT buying: guaranteed “penthouse” upside—without true penthouse features, you’re paying for marketing. If roof/elevator/build quality checks out, it’s a comfortable live-in pick; if not, walk fast.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (new build + playground + quieter edge-of-city vibe) Investor ➖ (rentability OK, but Durlești liquidity and finish-cost risk reduce IRR) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (views/parking/elevator are nice, but it’s not a walk-to-everything micro-area)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec list (underground parking, elevator to parking, monolithic frame) but you’re paying for “premium” marketing while taking on finishing risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €88,000–€93,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have real comps here). If they push “installments from developer,” negotiate the total price down anyway (rate terms can hide margin).

Bottom line

You are buying: a newer-build 3-room (~87 m²) in a managed residence with elevator + underground parking + quieter surroundings. You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment, nor a high-street, café-heavy walkable neighborhood — you’ll be driving/transiting for most daily life. Watch-outs: finish quality, noise insulation claims vs reality, and total all-in cost (apartment + parking + fit-out).

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances, autonomous heating) — but verify build quality/maintenance typical for “new construction” in the area.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (127 m², 3 bedrooms + living, near parks/schools) Investor ➖ (premium ticket size; works only if you believe in long-hold capital preservation + top-tier tenant demand) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Central, walkable, lots of cafes/parks; balcony; elevator)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great central micro-location and a big, usable layout, but the ask looks “trophy pricing”; you need proof (comps, building quality, HOA, parking reality) before paying full.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor hard on price-per-m²: at 127 m² and €550k you’re around €4.3k/m² (very premium for Chișinău unless the building is genuinely top-shelf). Comps are unknown from the data provided, so treat this as a hypothesis, not a fact. Target price range: €460k–€515k (roughly €3.6k–€4.1k/m²) unless the building/finishes/parking/security are clearly best-in-class. Ask for: building year/developer, monthly maintenance, heating costs, full legal pack, noise exposure, elevator condition, parking rules (is it truly assigned or just “open”).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, renovated, central family-sized apartment in a highly walkable pocket with parks and daily amenities. You are NOT buying: guaranteed “prime global-city” liquidity — resale depends heavily on building reputation, view/noise, and whether the renovation is actually high quality. You’re also not buying: certainty on parking value unless it’s deeded/allocated in writing.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.Parcare de tip deschis, teren de joacă pentru copii.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, equipped; low friction if you accept ground-floor living.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 100 m², 3 separated rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, close to schools/shops. Investor ➖ — rentable, but 1st floor + “fully furnished” pricing can cap upside; needs yield math. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — solid day-to-day convenience; not a “charming center” vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout in a newer building, but the 1st floor and agency listing mean you should push hard on price/terms.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €185,000–€195,000 (comps not provided, so treat this as a negotiation frame, not a valuation). Start ~€180k–€185k citing 1st-floor discount, noise/privacy risk, and “furnished” not equal to “higher market value”; be ready to settle closer to €190k–€195k if documents/condition are clean and parking terms are strong (subterranean spot included vs. optional).

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, efficient family layout (separate rooms, 2 baths) in a newer block with autonomous heating. You are NOT buying: top-tier privacy/quiet or premium resale liquidity—ground-floor units usually trade at a discount. If the underground parking is truly included and the renovation quality checks out, it becomes a practical “easy life” apartment rather than a romantic one.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 bedrooms + living + 2 bathrooms + playground/park proximity reads very family-forward. Investor ➖ — could rent well on size/layout, but finishing costs + higher ticket price make returns sensitive to rent level. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — 7/18 with elevator + autonomous heating + balcony suits someone who wants comfort and low hassle later.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is strong (110 sqm, 2 baths, autonomous heating, new build), but “white variant” shifts real total cost up; price only works if the building/location quality is proven.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on completion costs and uncertainty: ask for building documents (commissioning, management fees, utility setup), confirm actual delivered specs, and request a finishing allowance or price cut. Target price range: €140k–€150k (best-effort without comps; if the building is premium and views/noise are favorable, €150k–€154k may still be fair).

Bottom line

You are buying space and a modern building profile (elevator, autonomous heating) with a family-grade layout. You are NOT buying a turnkey apartment — finishing work, coordination, and “real total cost” are on you. Micro-area feels practical rather than trendy: daily needs look covered, nightlife/restaurant density looks limited.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~31.8/km², grocery density ~35.4/km², transit-stop density ~31.8/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Decebal is described as being in Botanica, about 3 km long, running from Piața Iulius Cezar to the intersection with bd. Dacia. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

Sources: Bulevardul Decebal din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

€185,000 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · str. Ialoveni
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — renovated, furnished, equipped, with autonomous heating; verify building docs + actual finish quality in person.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (91 m², 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, elevator, playground/parks nearby) Investor ➖ (good rentability on paper, but 185k purchase price can compress yield unless the exact micro-location is strong) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (lots of cafes/parks within a short walk; quick city access)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout + readiness are strong, but the price per m² feels “fully priced” unless the building quality and neighborhood pocket are top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €170,000–€180,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on (1) agency listing premium, (2) open parking vs secured/underground, (3) any building/HOA costs, (4) actual balcony/loggia situation (listing metadata conflicts), and (5) confirm autonomous heating legality + servicing. Ask for proof on: building commissioning, utilities contracts, and any mortgage/encumbrance history.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern-ish, family-sized, low-friction move-in apartment in a park-and-amenities dense pocket. You are NOT buying: guaranteed “quiet/low-traffic” living or a steal-priced deal; you’re paying for convenience and finish. If the building is solid and the street segment is calm, it’s a good home — just don’t overpay on the first ask.

(On-page characteristics)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Street (from listing fields): Ialoveni.

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper: renovated, furnished, appliances included; check workmanship + building systems.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, playground + park vibes, quieter street. Investor ➖ — rentable, but price-per-m² risk unless renovation quality is truly strong. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — good daily convenience without tourist noise; solid “liveable” neighborhood.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive package (renovated + furnished + elevator + parking), but you need to pressure-test the 64 m² layout and renovation quality to justify €125k.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112,000–€120,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: agency listing, “Euroreparație” ambiguity, open parking (not deeded/guaranteed), and 6/9 floor resale liquidity. Ask for: renovation year + receipts, utility bills, HOA/maintenance costs, and confirmation of elevator condition + building repairs plan.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, renovated, fully-equipped 3-room in a calm, park-adjacent micro-area with decent groceries/transit density. You are NOT buying: a central nightlife/walk-to-everything hotspot or a proven “no-questions” investment without comp support. If the layout feels tight at 64 m² or rooms are chopped up, the right move is price-first, not emotion-first.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“euro” finish), furnished + appliances, elevator building.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 4 rooms/78 m², 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies, parks/schools nearby. Investor ➖ — family rental could work, but Ciocana is more “local” demand than premium-expat; yield depends heavily on condition/building. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — practical day-to-day (groceries/transit/parks), less of a nightlife/old-town vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout + amenities, but price needs to be justified with comps for this street/building and true “4-room” reality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €108,000–€114,000 (best-effort guess; I do not have verified comps for this exact building/street). Push on: age/condition of the block, elevator/building maintenance, balcony condition, plumbing for 2 baths, and any hidden “cosmetic eurorepair” shortcuts. Ask for: cadastral extract, proof of area (78 m² what type), renovation invoices/photos, and a utility-bills snapshot.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical family-sized unit on a mid-floor in a 9-storey block with daily conveniences (parks, groceries, transit). You are NOT buying: a central/touristy location or a guaranteed “premium” resale story—this is value-through-function, not bragging-rights. If the building is well-managed and the layout is truly separate rooms (not a re-labeled plan), it’s a solid Ciocana family pick at the right price.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (121 m² + 2 baths + big terrace is a real family footprint, if schools/daycare access works for you)
Investor ➖ (new-build demand helps, but finish-cost + rentability depends heavily on micro-location and parking reality)
Lifestyle buyer ✔ (top floor + terrace + quieter green-ish complex vibe, assuming you like “residential campus” living)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong package on paper (top floor, big terrace, new-build spec), but you must price in finishing costs, rooftop/top-floor risks, and verify what’s actually delivered vs marketing.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for the exact deliverables in writing (utility hookups, glazing spec, elevator/roof warranties, terrace waterproofing details, parking terms). Use “grey shell” + top-floor heat/waterproofing risk + finish budget as leverage. Target price range: €130,000–€140,000 (best-effort; I don’t have true comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large top-floor 3-room shell in a master-planned complex with amenities and a terrace-led lifestyle.
You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort, guaranteed quiet/parking outcomes, or certainty until you verify build stage, handover timeline, and what “eco” features actually mean in bills and maintenance.

€189,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Armenească 47, Chișinău (sec. Centru)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (fresh renovation, furnished, reportedly never lived-in) — but verify workmanship + papers.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 separate bedrooms + living, garderobe, two balconies, 2/5 is a sweet spot) Investor ➖ (ultra-central rents well, but “3 bedrooms + living” can be niche; agency pricing risk) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Centru walkability, restaurants, parks density; easy city life)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great micro-location + turnkey renovation, but price needs to reflect unknowns (building type, parking, soundproofing, legal/tech condition).

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on the unknowns: building structure/maintenance ("cotileț" style can mean charming but uneven upkeep), insulation/noise (ultra-central), utilities/heating setup, and renovation documentation/warranties. Ask for: cadastral extract, layout plan, proof of 75 m², who did the renovation + receipts, and confirmation all alterations are registered. Target price range (best-effort guess, comps not provided): €165,000–€178,000. If everything checks out (quiet, solid building, clean docs, quality materials), stretching toward €180k+ is defensible; if any red flags (humidity, weak HOA, unregistered changes), push below €165k or walk.

Bottom line

You are buying a renovated, ultra-central, flexible layout that can work for a family or premium city rental. You are NOT buying certainty on building quality, parking, or long-term quiet without doing due diligence. At this price, the building and paperwork must be spotless — not just the photos.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on day 1 (cosmetic condition; already furnished + appliances) — but expect “agency staging”: inspect plumbing/ventilation + ground-floor humidity risk.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (80 m², 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, parks/schools nearby) Investor ➖ (good rentability, but 1st floor + unknown building condition caps premium) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (lots of nearby cafes/parks; ground-floor privacy/noise trade-off)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout + amenities density, but 1st floor in a 5-storey secondary stock building usually deserves a discount unless condition is truly clean.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €80,000–€85,000 (best-effort; no direct comps provided). Push on: 1st-floor discount, “cosmetic repair” (not a full renovation), verify building utilities/maintenance, and any signs of damp/mold. Ask for: last utility bills, renovation year, electrical panel status, and proof of legal layout for 2 bathrooms.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 3-room, 80 m² family apartment with 2 bathrooms and strong everyday convenience (parks + food/drink density + transit). You are NOT buying: a quiet, high-privacy unit — ground floor means more foot traffic, more noise sensitivity, and resale will depend heavily on smell/dampness and building upkeep.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Botanica.

Street (from listing fields): Chișinău.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~130.9/km², grocery density ~24.8/km², transit-stop density ~21.2/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

#54
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€199,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Albișoara, 82/8, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely move-in ready if the “new build” claim holds; confirm finish level + utilities/acceptance docs.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 bedrooms + living; city-center convenience) Investor ➖ (price-per-sqm likely high; yield depends on parking/finish/view) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (central, walkable, lots of daily amenities)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good central micro-location and a sensible 3-room layout, but the price looks “retail” and needs validation against finishes, noise, and building quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €180,000–€190,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Anchor with (1) Albisoara traffic/noise, (2) any missing details in the ad (parking, heating type, terrace, orientation), (3) corridor/common-area quality, (4) HOA costs + building management, (5) legal/technical file completeness. If it’s truly premium-finished and quiet (set back from the road) you may end closer to ask; if not, push hard.

Bottom line

You are buying: a central, amenity-dense “newer building” 3-room that should rent/sell easily later. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet or guaranteed top build quality—Albisoara can be loud and some “new builds” cut corners. If the apartment faces the boulevard, treat sound insulation as a make-or-break item.
