Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“eurorepair”), furnished, equipped, and high floor with elevator.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 73 m², 2 bathrooms, kid/pet-friendly, balcony, decent parks nearby. Investor ➖ — rentability could be fine, but check building type/age + noise/air quality on Muncești. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — strong everyday amenities, but this is more “practical city living” than cozy-quiet.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout and ready-to-live package, but the street/corridor risk (traffic/noise) + secondary stock should be priced with a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Start by treating the furniture/appliances as a convenience, not a premium (they depreciate). Push on “secondary building”, 8/9 floor realities (roof/elevator wear), and any road-facing noise. Target price range: €99,000–€105,000 (comps unknown; adjust if the block is truly well-managed and the apartment is quiet inside).

Bottom line

You are buying a turnkey, efficient 3-bedroom/2-bath setup with strong daily convenience (food, groceries, parks, transit). You are NOT buying a serene, low-traffic micro-location; the Muncești corridor can mean noise, dust, and resale sensitivity. Check elevator condition + building maintenance fund discipline — those decide whether “secondary stock” feels fine or feels tired.

€185,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · ул. Ион Буздуган, 11, Chișinău (Buiucani)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated, double-glazed), but verify building quality + ground-floor trade-offs.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (schools/parks/transit nearby; practical 86 m² layout) Investor ➖ (good liquidity area, but 1st-floor resale/rent discount vs mid-floors) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (quiet/residential feel; not a restaurant-heavy micro-pocket)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (3 rooms + living, 86 m², renovated) but the 1st floor should buy you a meaningful price concession.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on first-floor drawbacks (privacy, noise, security perception, light) and ask for documents that de-risk the “new construction” claim (commissioning, utilities, association fees, any known issues). Target price range: €165,000–€175,000 (comps unknown; adjust after you see 2–3 nearby sold/active 80–90 m² units and confirm the building’s true class/age).

Bottom line

You are buying: a spacious, family-oriented Buiucani apartment with a modern finish and everyday convenience. You are NOT buying: a bright, elevated, “premium feel” unit—first floor means you must be picky on noise, privacy, and building entrance hygiene. If the block is solid and the windows face a calm side, this can be a value play; if not, it becomes a perpetual negotiation with tenants/guests and resale buyers.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (furnished + appliances, double-glazed windows, armored door; elevator + intercom signals a maintained block).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies + nearby school/kindergarten/park/transit is the classic “daily life works” setup.
Investor ➖ — rentable on paper, but 5/9 in an older secondary block + unknown renovation depth = yields depend on condition/tenant segment.
Lifestyle buyer ➖ — green + connected, but this micro-pocket looks practical more than “buzzing” (low food/drink density in 300m).

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (69 m², 3 rooms, 2 baths, elevator, balconies) but you need to verify finish quality, noise, and building/HOA health before paying a full ask.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €88,000–€93,000. Use: (1) agency listing + duplicated fluff text, (2) secondary housing stock, (3) “move-in ready” often means cosmetic not capital works, (4) unknown sqm/floor in the structured fields (push for clarity + docs). Ask for: last major building repairs, elevator state, roof/waterproofing, heating setup, and utility bills.

Bottom line

You are buying: a pragmatic Botanica family apartment with real livability perks (2 bathrooms + 2 balconies, transit, parks, essentials nearby).
You are NOT buying: a central, restaurant-heavy, pedestrian-nightlife micro-area — it reads more calm/functional than vibrant.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” shell + autonomous heating + double glazing is a strong base, but assume you still need a real finish budget (floors/doors/kitchen/baths) unless proven otherwise.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 77 m², 2 bathrooms, playground, park-adjacent, lots of daily amenities nearby. Investor ➖ — good liquidity if priced right, but 11/15 + new-build shell means capex + resale depends on finish quality and building management. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — Botanica with high walkable density; easy public transport and plenty of cafés/parks in reach.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location signals are excellent and the layout spec is compelling, but “white variant” + unknown build quality/HOA/utility costs means the price needs to reflect your finishing and risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €140k–€148k (best-effort guess; I don’t have true comps here). Use (1) white-variant finishing costs, (2) elevator/management quality questions, (3) floor 11 exposure (wind/heat/pressure) as levers. Ask for: developer/house docs, utility bills estimate, sound insulation proof/feedback, and a detailed list of what “white variant” includes (wiring, heating boiler/radiators, plaster level, bathroom rough-ins).

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build, high-floor, amenity-dense Botanica base with autonomous heating and a practical 2-bath setup. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort (unless the finish is already done to a real standard) or guaranteed quiet/low monthly costs without verifying building management and construction quality. You’re paying for convenience + future finish flexibility — make sure the numbers still work after a realistic fit-out budget.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new build delivered Q3 2026 and sold as “white variant” (you still budget/time the full interior finish).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (closed yard + playground + parks signal, quieter setting) Investor ➖ (delivery risk + capital tied up; rentability depends on exact commute/amenities) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (modern complex, air/quiet, parking, floor heating — but not city-core buzz)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive developer product and pricing for a 3-room, but you’re buying future delivery + suburb feel (Stăuceni) while the listing labels “Centru”.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €100k–€105k (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted ask for delivery/finish-out and “last units” sales pressure). Push for developer concessions instead of just price: fixed-price finishing package, parking inclusion/discount (subterranean), clear specs on floor heating/autonomous heating, and penalty clauses for delivery delay. Verify what exactly is included in “white variant” (electrics, screed, plaster, boiler, radiators vs floor loops, doors/windows).

Bottom line

You are buying a small, club-house style new-build community near green space with modern systems and quieter living. You are NOT buying true city-center walkable daily life — current micro-amenities look thin (very few food/grocery within 300m). If commute to Chișinău fits your routine and you can handle delivery/fit-out risk, it’s a solid “suburban modern” play.

  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. #60
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€176,500 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · bd. Mircea cel Bătrân 39, Chișinău (Ciocana)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new renovation + furnished + A/C, with a practical 3BR + living layout.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 85–88 m², 2 bathrooms, storage, 4/9 floor: everyday-livable. Investor ➖ — rentable, but Ciocana is more “local demand” than prestige; yield depends heavily on exact building quality. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — decent restaurant/park access nearby, but this is not a nightlife / touristy core.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package in a workable micro-area, but price needs to be justified by building quality, exact layout, and finishing level.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €165k–€172k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps here, so treat this as a starting anchor, not a valuation). Ask for: (1) full plan + exact net area vs total, (2) building commissioning/acceptance status + any ongoing works, (3) monthly maintenance costs, (4) photos/video of common areas + elevator, (5) what exactly stays (appliances, furniture brands), (6) parking situation. Leverage: “developer listing” + furnished “new repair” often has markup; push on any vague specs (area discrepancy 85 vs 88 m², quality of finishes, noise/exposure).

Bottom line

You are buying a newer-build, mid-floor, family-friendly apartment with low friction move-in (furnishings + A/C + 2 baths). You are NOT buying a proven ‘prime central’ location; it’s more practical-city living than aspirational address. If the building is solid and the layout is efficient (no wasted corridors), it’s a good deal at the right number — not automatically at ask.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 6 grocery shops, 10 food/drink places, 53 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€275,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · strada Liviu Deleanu, 14/16, Chișinău (Buiucani)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“Euroreparație”), sold fully furnished/equipped; main unknown is building/roof/terrace waterproofing quality.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 bedrooms + separate kitchen, good day-to-day layout) Investor ➖ (premium price; terrace/penthouse narrows renter/buyer pool, but can rent well if finished right) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (top-floor privacy + big terrace is the whole point)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great product (top floor + terrace + turnkey), but you must price in terrace/roof risk and verify build quality; otherwise you’re overpaying for the vibe.

Negotiation strategy

Start by making the terrace/roof the central leverage: ask for documentation on roof works, terrace waterproofing details, any past leaks, and building maintenance reserves. Use a technical inspection to pressure a discount. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €245,000–€265,000 if condition/docs are solid; if any moisture/roof ambiguity appears, push closer to €230,000–€245,000 or walk.

Bottom line

You are buying: privacy, light, and an outdoor room (30 m² terrace) in a developed, non-touristy part of Buiucani. You are NOT buying: “set and forget” ownership—top-floor + terrace means ongoing diligence (waterproofing, insulation, wind/heat exposure). If the terrace is done right, it’s a daily quality-of-life upgrade; if not, it’s a recurring headache dressed as luxury.

#62
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€199,900 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · Chișinău, sectorul Ciocana, bd. Mircea cel Bătrân
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: modern renovation + furnished + appliances, plus autonomous heating and underfloor heating reads like “zero hassle” day‑1 living.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 2 bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground + park vibe; good everyday infrastructure nearby. Investor ➖ — liquid 3-room product, but pricing must be tight vs. Ciocana comps; cap rate likely not amazing at this ticket. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — big, bright, new-build feel; lots of parks and solid daily amenities, less about old-town café life.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready, new block, 100 m², 2 baths” package, but boulevard frontage + unknown building quality/association costs mean price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €180k–€192k (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted anchor). Push on (1) boulevard noise/dust, (2) parking being “open” not secured/owned, (3) agency “0% commission” still means they’re paid—use it to demand a cleaner price, (4) verify floor/heat bills/maintenance fees and any mortgage/encumbrance documents. If they won’t move on price, ask for inclusions (all furniture + appliances), minor fixes, and a faster notarization timeline.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, practical 3-room in a newer building—family-friendly layout with 2 bathrooms and comfort upgrades (autonomous + underfloor heat). You are NOT buying: a quiet, charming central neighbourhood experience; boulevard-adjacent living can trade convenience for noise. Treat it as a “high-comfort Ciocana base” and pay like one—no premium unless the building + orientation prove exceptional.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s a new-build “club house” concept but this unit is unfinished, so timeline/cost hinge on your fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (98 m², 2 bathrooms, terrace + courtyard/playground) Investor ➖ (developer sale/new build helps liquidity, but Telecentru here is more “liveable” than “tourist/prime rental”) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (quiet, low-density building, parking, panoramic glazing)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong low-rise/low-unit-count product with good specs, but unfinished delivery risk + price needs to reflect fit-out and any developer/paperwork unknowns.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €132k–€140k (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range vs. €147.3k list). Ask for: (1) clear delivery timeline + penalties, (2) what exactly is included in “unfinished” (utilities to door? screed? plaster? boiler?), (3) parking price/availability in writing, (4) warranty scope, (5) soundproofing spec proof, and (6) elevator/maintenance fee estimate.

Bottom line

You are buying a small, privacy-forward building (5 floors / 46 units) with autonomous heating + underfloor heating potential and family-friendly courtyard. You are NOT buying turnkey certainty — you’ll manage finishing costs, contractor coordination, and developer execution risk. If you negotiate properly, this can be a very comfortable long-term “live there” apartment rather than a pure yield play.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work (shell/unfinished): great if you want custom finishes; not great if you need to move fast.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies, playground, on-site parking) Investor ➖ (new build + prime crossroads is liquid, but premium pricing + unfinished narrows tenant pool) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (concierge + walkable daily stuff; strong “modern city living” vibe)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong package for Botanica (new premium tower, amenities, good daily walkability), but it’s developer pricing for an unfinished unit, so your edge is in terms, timing, and upgrades.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €145,000–€155,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have fresh comps for Etalon / Decebal–Dacia). Push on: (1) finishing credit or turnkey option, (2) parking inclusion/discount (subterranean spot is often where margin hides), (3) payment schedule milestones, (4) snagging/warranty terms, (5) floorplan orientation and window-to-wall ratio (panoramic glass can mean heat/glare/noise exposure).

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern high-rise lifestyle product—concierge, underground parking, smart-home wiring, and a very convenient Botanica node. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in, quiet/low-traffic serenity (crossroads location), or a “cheap sqm” deal. If the layout/orientation is good and the developer’s execution is real, this can be a flagship Botanica apartment—just don’t overpay for the word “premium.”

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (high-spec finish + furnished/with appliances; expect only taste-level tweaks).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (space, 3 bathrooms, closed courtyard + playground) Investor ➖ (premium ticket; depends on luxury-rental demand + HOA/maintenance) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (penthouse feel, terrace, central walkability, parking)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong product (145 m², terrace, new build, parking/secure courtyard) but the ask is “top of market”; you want leverage on price and running costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €450k–€480k. Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, central, two-level “statement” apartment that lives like a small house (terrace + multiple bathrooms + wardrobes). You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-maintenance, ultra-liquid unit — premium layouts can be harder to resell and can carry higher upkeep.

Also noted: “Oferim spre vânzare penthouse în 2 nivele în complexul locativ construit de compania Dream Home Residence, amplasat în sectorul Centru, str. Moara Roșie. Locuința se desfășoară pe o suprafață de 145 m2, localizat la e…”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €499 000. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Etaj, Euroreparație, Fond locativ, Living, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Starea apartamentului, Suprafață totală. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În imediata apropiere se regăsesc diverse magazine alimentare și de uz casnic, sucursale bancare, farmacii, restaurante, grădiniță, școală.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Centru.

Street (from listing fields): Moara Roșie.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~130.9/km², grocery density ~24.8/km², transit-stop density ~21.2/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

  • Price: €499,000
  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Centru
#66
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€89,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · bd. Dacia, sect. Botanica
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — explicitly “needs repair”, so budget/time for a full refresh.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 separate rooms + 2 bathrooms is a practical layout. Investor ➖ — works if you can renovate efficiently, but central heating + busy boulevard micro-noise can cap rent premium. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — good city access, less “charming-stroll” vibe right on a main artery.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (64 m², high floor, 2 bathrooms, parking mentioned) but renovation + likely traffic/noise risk should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Start from the “needs repair” angle and push hard on total renovation cost + disruption (13/20 means elevator reliance during works). Target price range: €78,000–€84,000 (best-effort; I don’t have verified comps for bd. Dacia/this building series). Ask for: (1) clear confirmation of heating costs/terms for central heating, (2) recent utility bills, (3) any building/elevator capex plans, (4) exact parking status (reserved vs “open/first come”).

Bottom line

You are buying a practical family layout in a high-rise on a major Botanica corridor with strong everyday access. You are NOT buying turnkey comfort — renovation and boulevard-side noise/air quality are the likely trade-offs. If you renovate well, it becomes a solid “workhorse” home rather than a romantic one.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Apartament cu planimetrie practică, potrivit pentru familie. Amplasare excelentă pe una dintre principalele artere ale sectorului Botanica, cu acces rapid la transport public, centre comerciale, școli și alte facilită…”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 8 grocery shops, 3 food/drink places, 66 parks/playgrounds, and 8 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Dacia is described as the main artery of the Botanica sector, running from Piața Ovidiu to șoseaua Muncești and towards the airport area; the article also mentions the City Gates („Porțile Orașului”) mark the entry into the city from the airport direction. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Dacia din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

Sources: Bulevardul Dacia din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

#67
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€212,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Chișinău, Botanica, ул. Гренобле
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: furnished, quality appliances, autonomous heating, AC, elevator; layout is already “done” (no obvious renovation signal).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (130 m² + 2 baths + 2-level separation works; playground mentioned) Investor ➖ (premium ticket size; duplex layouts can narrow tenant/buyer pool later) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (top-floor-ish feel, privacy by levels, turnkey)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong turnkey duplex (130 m², 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies, parking) but Botanica micro-location here reads more “green/residential” than “walk-to-restaurants,” and duplex resale liquidity is usually lower.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €195k–€205k (best-effort; I don’t have verified comps for this building/street). Anchor on (1) duplex complexity/maintenance, (2) any elevator/roof/top-floor noise risk at 10/11, (3) confirm parking is actually usable/assigned (not just “open”), and (4) validate monthly costs for autonomous heating + building admin. If documents/condition are flawless and parking is truly secured, expect to land closer to €205k.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern, two-level “house-in-the-sky” that’s ready to live in immediately. You are NOT buying: a café-and-nightlife walkable hotspot; expect more car/transit dependence for evening buzz. Your leverage: premium price + niche layout + top-floor risk = discount, even when the interior is great.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Sculeni, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Sculeni, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€285,000 · 3 rooms · Sculeni · strada Calea Ieșilor, Sculeni, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (marketed as repaired + furnished; autonomous heating is a real comfort upgrade).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 bedrooms + balcony + playground nearby; high floor helps with noise) Investor ➖ (premium price point; yield depends heavily on actual finishes/parking/building fees) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (panoramic windows + developed infrastructure; quick access toward center via Calea Ieșilor)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good “new-build, high-floor, turnkey” package, but the ask looks like it’s priced for perfect condition/location and you should verify build quality + true layout.

Negotiation strategy

Start by stress-testing the listing’s “3 rooms” claim (it reads like 3 bedrooms + separate kitchen), then use objective hooks: floor 11/13 (wind/elevator dependence), monthly maintenance/association fees, and any missing essentials (parking, storage, sound insulation). Target price range (best-effort): €255,000–€270,000. If comparable sales in Sculeni/new builds confirm the premium and the renovation is genuinely high-end, expect them to defend closer to €275k–€285k.

Bottom line

You are buying a high-floor, newer-build, turnkey family apartment on a main corridor with strong daily convenience. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet (arterial road), nor guaranteed “luxury” unless the finishes, windows, and building engineering check out in person. If the building is solid and the interior is actually premium, it’s a comfortable long-term hold — but don’t pay top-of-market without comps and a technical sniff test.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€166,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · Chișinău, sect. Buiucani, str. Vasile Lupu
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” finish usually means you still need a full interior fit‑out (floors, doors, kitchen, bathrooms finishes) before living comfortably.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 92 m², 2 bathrooms, lots of nearby parks + schools/daycare mentioned. Investor ➖ — rentable area, but 1st floor + no balcony can narrow tenant demand and pricing. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — good daily convenience (groceries/food nearby), but missing balcony and likely less privacy on floor 1.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and neighborhood convenience, but the finish stage (“white variant”), 1st floor, and no balcony should translate into a meaningful discount.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor hard on (1) remaining fit‑out costs for a 92 m² unit, (2) 1st-floor liquidity/privacy penalty, and (3) no balcony. Ask for building details that move price: exact building class/hand-over status, window orientation, noise, and any land/maintenance fees. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps unknown): €145,000–€158,000. If they can document premium build quality and quiet orientation, you may accept closer to ask; otherwise keep walking.

Bottom line

You are buying space and a practical family layout in a park-heavy, convenience-rich micro-area. You are NOT buying turnkey comfort — expect time + cash to finish it, and you won’t have balcony utility. First floor can be a bargain if it’s quiet and secure; if not, it’s a long-term resale drag.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 bedrooms + living, 2 baths, terrace, playground, parks nearby) Investor ➖ (sellable layout, but 1st floor + quieter amenity density can cap rent premium) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (more “calm residential” than “walk to bars/coffee every night”)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid spec (89 m², 2 baths, terrace, new-build vibe), but 1st floor + thin nearby daily amenities/transit suggest the price needs to be sharp.

Negotiation strategy

Start by treating this as a “great apartment, slightly compromised position” deal: 1st floor (privacy/noise/security perceptions) is your leverage even in a good building. Target price range: €175k–€190k (best-effort; true comps not provided). If they won’t move much on price, push for value: included furniture/appliances confirmed in contract, parking rights clarified, and a quick-close discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a family-ready, renovated 3-room with terrace and 2 bathrooms in a quieter, park-adjacent pocket. You are NOT buying: a high-floor light/view apartment or a “step outside into cafes + transit” micro-location. If you’ll drive and want calm + space, it can be a strong buy—at the right number.

Family ✔ — 3 separate bedrooms + big living/kitchen, 3 bathrooms helps daily life. Investor ➖ — trophy rental potential, but niche demand + high ticket may mean longer vacancy/resale cycles. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — central, restaurant-dense micro-area + terrace + penthouse feel.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — quality/fit looks strong, but at this price you must validate building management, noise/traffic, and resale liquidity before paying “trophy pricing.”

Negotiation strategy

Ask for hard proof: exact floor/total floors, building year/type, monthly maintenance/HOA, heating system specs and bills, parking legal status (owned vs assigned), terrace waterproofing warranty, and any permits for interior changes. Use “liquidity + verification” as leverage: penthouses take longer to move, and buyers will discount uncertainty. Target price range: €440k–€475k (best-effort). If comps for this building/streets confirm true top-of-market trades, you may have to live closer to ask; comps are not provided here, so assume negotiation room.

Bottom line

You are buying a high-spec, high-comfort central penthouse with real “daily luxury” (3 baths, storage, terrace) and a walkable, amenity-rich micro-area. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet or easy resale—central zones plus niche pricing can amplify noise sensitivity and time-on-market risk. If the building/parking/terrace paperwork is clean, this is a lifestyle win; if not, the premium finishes won’t save the deal.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (94 m², 3 bedrooms, playground + nearby schools/park) Investor ➖ (good “ready-to-rent” setup, but price level may compress yield) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (more practical residential than “late-night walkable”; some transit + basics nearby)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong, family-sized unit in a newer building and turnkey finish, but the €210k ask needs comp support and a careful building/management check.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €195k–€205k (best-effort without comps; if finishes/building are truly top-tier and paperwork is clean, expect tighter discount). Ask for: proof/receipts for renovation + appliances, exact maintenance fees, winter heating costs (autonomous system), elevator status/maintenance, parking reality (is it assigned or “first come”), and noise/wind exposure on floor 10.

Bottom line

You are buying: a turnkey, family-oriented 3-room setup in Botanica with modern basics (elevator, balcony, intercom, autonomous heat). You are NOT buying: a proven “cheap” deal or a nightlife-centric micro-area; value hinges on build quality, views/noise, and comps. Check: building developer reputation, association governance, and any roof/elevator/utility issues typical for 10/14-floor blocks.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build “white finish” means you still budget time + cash for full interior finishing.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (big layout, 2 bathrooms, planned kid infrastructure, quieter setting) Investor ➖ (new-build liquidity can be fine, but amenity density looks weak right around the site) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (more “calm residential” than “walk out for coffee and dinner”)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong size/specs on paper, but top-floor risks + developer-marketing vibes + unclear immediate daily-walk amenities.

Negotiation strategy

Start by treating this as: price for shell + finishing + parking decisions. Ask what exactly is included in the “white finish” (electrics, screed, heating loops, boiler, doors, insulation spec) and whether the listed price includes VAT/fees and the underground spot. Target price range: €99k–€106k (best-effort; comps not provided). If they won’t move on price, push for value: underground parking included/discounted, finishing credit, or a staged payment plan with penalties for delays.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3BR+living in a low-rise new-build with 2 bathrooms, terrace/balconies, and a “quiet residential complex” proposition. You are NOT buying: proven neighborhood buzz or a highly walkable daily-life setup right at your door. Top-floor living can be great — but only if the terrace/waterproofing, roof insulation, and elevator reliability are genuinely solid.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 6/15, playground mention, schools/shops nearby) Investor ➖ (good size, but capex + finish risk; depends on rent market for new-build shells) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (practical micro-area; not a café/transit hotspot based on nearby counts)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid new-build 3-room at a workable price point, but finishing costs + unknown building/maintenance details should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Start by pricing the “white-box gap”: ask exactly what is included (walls/leveling, wiring, plumbing points, radiators/boiler, doors, bathroom rough-ins), plus monthly maintenance and any outstanding developer fees. Use any missing specifics (no clear floorplan here; listing aggregator; agency listing) as leverage. Target price range (best-effort): €98,000–€103,000. If the unit is truly turnkey-legal (all docs clean) and the building has strong management, stretch closer to ask; if finishing scope is bigger than expected, push lower. Comparable sales not provided here, so treat this as a risk-adjusted anchor, not a comp-based valuation.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large (≈86 m²) new-build 3-room shell with autonomous heating and elevator convenience. You are NOT buying: a finished home, a proven “walk-to-everything” micro-location, or certainty on total budget until you quantify the fit-out. Finishing budget discipline is the whole game on this one—get a contractor quote before you commit.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€224,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Vasile Lupu, sec. Buiucani, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” means you’re buying a shell and paying (time + cash) to finish it properly.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + 112 m² + 2 bathrooms + park-heavy micro-area reads like comfortable day-to-day living. Investor ➖ — large unit and fit-out capex can compress yields unless you have a clear premium-rental angle. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — quieter/greener feel; dining/nightlife density nearby looks modest.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (size, new build, Buiucani, mid-floor) but the finish cost + developer/HOA unknowns should translate into a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €200k–€215k (best-effort; I don’t have local closed-sale comps here, so treat this as a leverage anchor, not a valuation). Push on: (1) white-variant finishing budget and timeline, (2) any developer-delivery snags, (3) elevator/parking/HOA details, (4) documentation readiness. If they won’t move on price, ask for concessions (parking spot, finishing credit, or clear delivery/acceptance terms).

Bottom line

You are buying space and a newer-building baseline in a green, practical pocket (groceries + transit are present). You are NOT buying “move-in ready” — you’re committing to a full interior project and the quality risks that come with it. If you want turnkey simplicity, this is the wrong listing; if you want to control the final product, it can be a smart play at the right price.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 9 grocery shops, 5 food/drink places, 189 parks/playgrounds, and 11 transit stops. On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · ул. Василе Лупу, 28, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (fresh renovation claims), but verify build quality, plumbing/electrics, and any renovation paperwork.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (schools/kindergarten nearby + functional 3-room + living layout) Investor ➖ (rentability depends on building reputation/parking; price-per-sqm likely on the punchy side) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (good green space + transit, but not a “walk-out-into-cafés” micro-area from the POI signals)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong practical location and a usable layout, but the ask looks premium for 71 sqm on a low floor unless the building is top-tier and impeccably maintained.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on €/sqm logic: 185k for 71 sqm implies ~2,600 €/sqm (comps unknown). Push for a target range of 165,000–175,000 € unless they can prove standout advantages (excellent building management, quiet courtyard orientation, secure parking, low utility costs, no legal/technical issues). Demand: maintenance fees/association details, elevator/service history, heating type and winter bills, and confirmation of ownership/docs; use any uncertainty to justify price reduction.

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, family-practical apartment in Buiucani with solid transit access and lots of nearby green space. You are NOT buying: a guaranteed “prime, lively” pedestrian micro-location or an automatic investment slam-dunk at this price. Low floor means: easier daily life (stairs backup) but typically less privacy and more street/entrance noise risk—orientation matters.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (87 m², 3 rooms + living, parks/schools nearby) Investor ➖ (price looks premium; depends on true rent level + building quality) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (quiet/green micro-area; dining/nightlife density looks modest)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid layout + floor 2 in a newer block, but the ask price reads “top of market” for the amenity mix shown.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for the full spec pack (year of build, developer, insulation, heating type, monthly maintenance, parking, elevator count, cadastral docs). Then negotiate on price-per-m²: at 235k for 87 m² you’re roughly in the ~2.7k €/m² zone, which is often a premium unless the building/finishes/parking are truly A-tier. Target price range: €205,000–€225,000 (best-effort estimate; I don’t have local closed-sale comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, family-leaning apartment in a green, practical part of Buiucani with good transit reach. You are NOT buying: a hyper-urban, restaurant-heavy micro-location—or a slam-dunk “can’t miss” deal at this sticker price. If the building is genuinely high-quality (construction, heating, soundproofing, parking), it can justify more; if not, the price should come down.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — modern building, “Euro renovation,” sold furnished/equipped, autonomous heating + warm floors.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 97 m², 3 rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, warm/quiet “middle” unit, parks nearby. Investor ➖ — easy to rent due to size/condition, but this is a premium ticket for Ciocana; yield may be just OK. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — high-floor view and sun exposure are nice; the micro-area reads practical more than “boutique/café”.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the apartment spec is strong (97 m², renovated, furnished, high floor, 4 elevators), but you need to confirm build quality, monthly maintenance, and noise/wind reality at floor 14 before paying top money.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235,000–€250,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) high-floor risks (wind/noise/elevator dependency), (2) “open” parking (not guaranteed), (3) verify renovation receipts/brands and warranty status, (4) confirm heating costs + actual building energy performance, (5) confirm legal status of “private yard” and what you truly get access to.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, efficient-feeling family apartment that’s already finished and equipped. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/low-wind comfort at height, or a locked-in parking solution, or a proven ‘best deal’ without comp validation.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” shell means you’re buying a project, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + 2 bathrooms + schools/services nearby, but stairs to 6–7th can be a daily tax) Investor ➖ (rentability depends on finishing cost + tenant tolerance for no elevator) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (duplex vibe is cool, but the walk-up factor and finish-out time reduce spontaneity)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great headline price for 77 m² and 3 rooms, but no elevator + top-floor duplex + renovation budget should be priced in hard.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on: (1) no elevator to floors 6–7, (2) finish-out cost/risk (white variant), (3) potential heat/roof/top-floor issues and resale friction. Target price range: €43,000–€47,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps from this exact micro-area/building type). Ask for: confirmation of roof condition/any past leaks, insulation, heating setup, legal/technical docs for the 2-level configuration, and a realistic all-in estimate for finishing.

Bottom line

You are buying: a cheap-per-m² duplex layout with 2 bathrooms and decent everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: elevator convenience, turnkey livability, or an easy future resale to every buyer segment. If you can handle stairs and you have a controlled renovation plan, it can be a value play; otherwise it’s a fatigue generator.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white variant” means you’re buying a shell with good bones, then paying (time + money) to finish it properly.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + closed courtyard/playground is a strong daily-life setup. Investor ➖ — premium price point + high-floor liquidity risk; could work if the complex is truly top-tier and finishes are kept sensible. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — top-floor/terrace vibe, quiet above neighbors; you’ll trade some convenience for that.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great “new-build, top-floor, 95 m², 2 baths” package, but the 17/17 + unfinished state should be priced with a real discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235k–€250k (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). You are NOT buying: a turnkey home (finish costs are real) or a “forever quiet” guarantee (top-floor wind/heat + elevator dependence + terrace upkeep). If the terrace/roof details are clean and the finishing budget stays disciplined, it can be a high-comfort place; if not, it becomes an expensive project.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Rîșcani (sector) (Wikipedia, ro)

#83
Apartament cu 4 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 4 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€138,000 · 4 rooms · Râșcani · Chișinău, sect. Râșcani, str. Miron Costin
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances; expect only inspection-driven fixes).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 4 rooms, 100 m², 2 bathrooms, 2 enclosed balconies, park nearby. Investor ➖ — good demand area, but top-floor liquidity + price sensitivity may cap yield. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — pleasant/quiet + amenities, but top floor can be a daily tax if no elevator.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional family layout and micro-location signals, but 5/5 top-floor risk (heat/roof, stairs/elevator unknown) should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on top-floor + building/roof condition + elevator uncertainty + “eurorepair” quality variance. Ask for: roof maintenance history, insulation/overheating proof, utility bills (autonomous heating), HOA/security fees, and a daylight/ventilation check in all rooms. Target price range: €125k–€132k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). If they can document recent roof work + low bills + solid building management/security, you can accept closer to ask.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, ready-to-live family apartment in Râșcani with park-side orientation and strong everyday convenience. You are NOT buying: a “set-and-forget” unit—top-floor building risk and comfort (summer heat, roof) must be verified. If the building is well-run and the roof is proven solid, this becomes a very practical long-term home.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: cosmetic renovation + partial furniture; expect some refresh depending on taste.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, 3 balconies, playground, elevator) Investor ➖ (panel block + 8th floor can narrow renter/buyer pool; still strong practical layout) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (good everyday convenience, but Dacia boulevard likely means more traffic/noise)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional package (72 m², 2 bathrooms, insulation/radiators, elevator) but panel building + high floor + boulevard exposure should buy you a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a price concession based on (1) panel building, (2) cosmetic-only finish, (3) 8/9 floor dependence on elevator, and (4) likely noise/dust from bd. Dacia. Target price range: €85,000–€90,000 (best-effort; without verified comps, treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying space efficiency: 72 m² with real family ergonomics (2 bathrooms + storage + balconies) in a transit-served pocket. You are NOT buying a quiet, boutique building experience; this reads like pragmatic “good bones” city living. If the view/light is solid and the balconies aren’t compromised, this can be a very livable Botanica base — at the right price.

Bulevardul Dacia is described as the main artery of the Botanica sector, running from Piața Ovidiu to șoseaua Muncești and towards the airport area; the article also mentions the City Gates („Porțile Orașului”) mark the entry into the city from the airport direction. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

€185,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Vasile Lupu, 28, sectorul Buiucani, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating + AC.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, closed courtyard + playground, schools nearby. Investor ➖ — rentable, but price point likely needs sharp underwriting and building/HOA clarity. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — mid-floor, double-sided layout, quiet-courtyard vibe with decent daily conveniences.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package in Buiucani, but you should pressure-test the €/m² and building/maintenance details before paying a premium.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €170,000–€178,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so validate against recent sales in Buiucani new-builds). Use the seller’s “credit with 30% down” pitch as leverage (it signals they want volume/velocity). Ask for: proof of 71 m² measured area (gross vs net), exact room count definition ("3 + living" can hide a small bedroom), monthly maintenance/HOA fees, elevator uptime/service contract, courtyard/parking rules (allocated vs first-come), and any outstanding developer/utility issues. If paperwork is clean, trade price for speed: fast notary date + minimal conditions.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, family-friendly, closed-courtyard new-build unit with autonomous heating and a practical layout. You are NOT buying a nightlife/tourist micro-location; it’s more calm-residential than “step outside into a packed restaurant street.” You’re paying for convenience (finished + furnished) — make sure the finish quality and building management actually justify it.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (modern individual design; layout already optimized for daily living).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + terrace/green outlook is a strong everyday-life setup) Investor ➖ (could rent well, but price-per-sqm risk is high until you confirm total area and building class) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (quiet micro-area, terrace, and lots of nearby green space signals a “live well” apartment)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — looks like a premium “ready to live” unit, but the listing is missing the two numbers that decide everything: total sqm and exact building type/quality.

Negotiation strategy

Start by forcing clarity: request official total sqm (incl. terrace/balconies), cadastral extract, building year/class, heating/utility scheme, and parking terms (owned vs rented). Target price range: €255k–€275k as an opening/closing band (best-effort guess; comps unknown without sqm). If the area is large and the terrace is genuinely usable + parking is included as property, accept nearer ask; if sqm is modest or terrace is “paper terrace”, press harder.

Bottom line

You are buying: a calm Buiucani base with good everyday amenities, strong greenery nearby, and a modern finished interior. You are NOT buying: a guaranteed “great deal” until the sqm, building quality, and parking/legal terms are verified. This can be a sleeper-great home — or an overpriced finish-job — depending on the missing specs.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — “Euro renovation” + built-in furniture/appliances + modern systems reads like a turnkey high-floor unit.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (4 separate bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + big living/kitchen zone) Investor ➖ (price suggests luxury end; resale/rent works only if the building + parking + docs are clean) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (panoramic windows, high-floor views, good daily convenience)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/specs are strong, but €398k in Botanica needs the building story (construction quality, management fees, parking, paperwork) to justify it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €350k–€380k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Anchor on: unknown building class/age, 12/16 floor liquidity risk, any noise from Dacia Blvd, and require proof of autonomous heating legality + renovation receipts/warranties. Make the offer conditional on: full ownership docs, no encumbrances, technical passport matching the current layout, and a clean utilities/association statement.

Bottom line

You are buying a large, modern-feeling family apartment with real separation (4 bedrooms) and “premium comfort” features (heated floors, autonomous heating, branded kit). You are NOT buying quiet suburbia — Dacia Blvd can mean traffic/noise; high-floor living also means elevator dependency. At this price, the building’s quality, maintenance, and parking situation matter almost as much as the apartment itself.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper for a new-build (autonomous heating, elevator, security), but verify actual finishing package, terrace waterproofing, and handover timeline/defects.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 bedrooms + lots of bathrooms/loggias; but top-floor wind/heat and school-run logistics matter. Investor ➖ — 4-room/penthouse is niche; can rent well, but liquidity on resale is slower than mid-market 2–3 rooms. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — terrace + panoramic vibe + winter-garden space is the whole point.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — compelling layout and size for the price, but Poșta Veche micro-location looks quiet and the “developer listing” risk means you must buy only after paperwork + build quality checks.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €145k–€152k (best-effort; true comps unknown). Ask for: (1) written finishing spec (floors, doors, bathroom fit-out), (2) warranty terms for roof/terrace and façade, (3) confirmation of elevator servicing + building management fees, (4) bundle/discount for underground parking, (5) snag list + retention holdback until defects fixed.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, top-floor “penthouse-like” new-build with multiple outdoor spaces and a flexible 2-living layout. You are NOT buying: a walk-out-the-door restaurant street or high-tourist buzz; the immediate 300m POI profile reads residential/low-amenity. Treat terrace + top-floor as the main risk surface: waterproofing, heat gain, wind, noise, and elevator reliability.

Anchor hard on renovation uncertainty + unknown building/utility capex (roof/risers/stairwell) and request proof of heating system condition. Target price range: €82k–€90k (comps not provided; treat as a negotiation band, not a valuation).

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, autonomous heating, sold furnished/with appliances).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 rooms, 2nd floor, parks/transit/shops density suggests daily convenience) Investor: ➖ (good location liquidity, but yield unknown without sqm + rent comps) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Centru walkability vibe; lots of food/drink nearby)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “easy life” package in Centru, but price is impossible to validate without total sqm and building condition details.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105,000–€112,000 (best-effort guess; comps/sqm unknown). Anchor your offer on (1) missing/unclear total area, (2) 5-floor walk-up dynamics (noise/maintenance/stairwell), (3) verify autonomous heating legality/service history, (4) insulation/renovation quality proof (photos, invoices), and (5) building capital repairs fund / HOA situation. If the sqm turns out small for a 3-room, push harder.

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, turnkey 3-room in a central, amenity-dense micro-area with winter comfort upside (autonomous heating). You are NOT buying: a fully “risk-free” deal until you confirm sqm, building type/condition, and the heating system’s documentation. If paperwork + sqm check out, this is the kind of Centru apartment that’s easy to live in and relatively easy to resell.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” in a new-build usually means you’re buying a high-quality shell + systems, but you still need full finishes (floors/doors/kitchen/baths details).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 87 m², 2 bathrooms, balconies, elevator, playground, underground parking reads like a practical live-in layout. Investor ➖ — new-build + good spec helps rentability, but ground/low floor and unknown net-to-gross + fees can compress yields. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — solid comfort package, but this isn’t the “walk out into buzzing streets” type micro-area; more calm/residential.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the build spec and developer pitch sound strong, but the low/ground level and “not finished” status are your leverage; verify noise/privacy and the true finishing scope.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €121k–€128k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Ask for either a direct discount or developer-paid upgrades (bathroom rough-ins confirmed, better door package, AC pre-wiring, parking discount). Use: (1) low/ground floor, (2) finishing budget uncertainty, (3) any view/privacy compromises. If they won’t move on price, push for extras and a clear delivery/hand-over checklist.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern new-build unit with strong “spec-sheet” signals (monolithic frame, insulated facade, premium elevator, autonomous gas boiler) in a green-ish, serviceable Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: instant move-in simplicity or guaranteed quiet/privacy — the floor level can be the make-or-break, and finishing costs/timelines are on you unless negotiated. You are also not buying: a touristy/restaurant-dense core; this looks more residential with decent groceries + transit access.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build unit, “Euro” finish, furnished + appliances, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 separate bedrooms + living; playground; lots of green nearby) Investor ➖ (turnkey helps, but price-per-sqm looks premium for Buiucani unless building/finish is truly top-tier) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (high floor, panoramic glazing vibe, forest-facing feel, low-tourism micro-area)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready to live” package and view/height, but you’re paying for finish + new-build hype; make the price prove itself.

Negotiation strategy

List is 204,900€ for 74 m² (~2,770€/m²). Without verified comps for this exact ExFactor complex, assume negotiation is possible: target 185,000–198,000€ depending on what’s included (furniture/appliances), building quality, and any hidden costs (maintenance fees, parking, documents). Ask for: proof of total area, utility bills for winter (autonomous heating claims), and a snag list (windows/condensation, ventilation, elevator noise, water pressure at floor 9).

Bottom line

You are buying a high-floor, turnkey 3-room in a greener Buiucani pocket with a “new + finished” premium. You are NOT buying a central/foot-traffic location (POI density for food/drink is modest; transit count looks limited). If the view and finish quality are real, it’s a comfortable long-hold; if not, this is overpriced by the m².

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“Euroreparație”), furnished + appliances, elevator building; likely only cosmetic tweaks if your taste differs.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + parks/playground + schools nearby) Investor ➖ (solid rental appeal in Buiucani, but agency “investment” pitch may mean price is already sharpened) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (lots of food/drink + parks within a short walk; good daily-life density)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and micro-area amenities, but you should treat “ready + investment” marketing as a sign to pressure-test build quality, noise, and price-per-m².

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105,000–€112,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have confirmed comps or an on-page condition audit). Push on: (1) any evidence of rushed renovation, (2) elevator/roof/common-area condition in a 9-floor block, (3) autonomous heating service history, (4) parking reality ("open" often means “hunt-for-a-spot”). If they won’t move on price, ask for seller-paid notary/registration costs or leaving all furniture/appliances with written inventory.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical family layout (2 baths + 2 balconies) in a walkable Buiucani pocket with lots of parks and everyday services. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet, guaranteed parking, or guaranteed “premium” renovation—verify workmanship, building maintenance, and neighborhood noise patterns before committing.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/features read “premium family stock”, but price needs validation against comps + building pedigree (new-build quality varies).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €175k–€188k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: cosmetic renovation + furnished + autonomous heating usually means “liveable now, upgrade later.”

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 59 m², 2 bathrooms, floor 3/5; daily life convenience looks strong. Investor ➖ — rentable, but upside depends on actual layout quality + building condition; verify documents and utilities. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — solid practical location; not a “boutique central” vibe (good amenities, low tourism).

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional package (59 m², 3/5, balcony, 2 baths, autonomous heat) but price needs to reflect cosmetic finish and secondary stock reality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €88,000–€93,000 (comps unknown; this is a best-effort anchor). Push on: “cosmetic renovation” (expect near-term spend), any heating system age/service records, roof/stairwell condition, and the fact it’s secondary stock. Ask for: recent utility bills, HOA/maintenance payments status, and a short list of included furniture/appliances.

Bottom line

You are buying a practical, family-friendly Botanica base with strong day-to-day walkability (food, groceries, parks, transit nearby). You are NOT buying a premium-finish, zero-surprises apartment—cosmetic work + secondary building means inspection and paperwork matter. This is a “live now, optimize later” deal if the structure/layout checks out.

You are NOT buying: a super-liquid, easiest-to-resell standard unit; duplex + Durlești can take longer to exit and buyers will scrutinize build quality and documents.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong product (top-floor + terrace + new build) but the price needs to reflect the remaining fit-out and any penthouse/top-floor risks.

Bottom line

You are NOT buying: a turnkey interior (budget meaningful extra) or guaranteed quiet/comfort unless the terrace insulation + elevator + roof details check out. If parking matters, price it in now because it’s not included.

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Zonă dezvoltată, aproape de centre comerciale, farmacii, școli, grădinițe, transport public și acces rapid spre toate direcțiile orașului.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 3 grocery shops, 7 food/drink places, 3 parks/playgrounds, and 0 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Sector (from listing fields): Centru.

Street (from listing fields): Nicolae Testemițanu.

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

#100
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · strada Ion Buzdugan 13, Buiucani, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: furnished, “quality renovation,” autonomous heating + underfloor heating reads like a finished, comfort-forward setup.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + closed courtyard + playground is the classic family stack. Investor ➖ — good liquidity if priced right, but ground floor + agency retail pricing can cap upside. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — Buiucani is generally a strong “liveable” choice; underground parking option helps.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec list in a new-build, but it’s 1st floor and the ask is ambitious unless finishing + layout + building quality truly stand out in person.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort; comps unknown): €165k–€178k. Start around €160k–€165k anchored on (a) 1st-floor discount, (b) any noise/privacy risk, (c) verify actual usable area, finishing quality, and parking terms. Ask explicitly: is parking included or extra, what’s the monthly maintenance, and any waterproofing/history for ground/first-floor units.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, family-oriented new-build package: 3BR + 2 baths + balcony + autonomous comfort systems. You are NOT buying “quiet, private, above-it-all” — first floor means more exposure to courtyard/entry traffic and more downside if the building management isn’t tight. If the building is genuinely well-insulated and the courtyard is well-run, this can be a very practical long-term home.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Râșcani · bd. Renașterii Naționale, 4, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (positioned as premium/fully finished) — verify what “ultrafinished” includes in writing (kitchen, appliances, lighting, AC, wardrobes).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 74 m², low-ish floor, parks nearby; check storage + noise. Investor ➖ — good liquidity if truly turnkey, but new-build pricing can cap yield. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — “Circ” area gives city energy + transit; not a sleepy suburb vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” new-build pitch and a practical 2/9 floor, but price hinges on finish level, view/noise (boulevard), and building quality/HOA costs.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: exact net vs gross area split, full spec sheet (materials/brands), warranty/acceptance docs, monthly maintenance estimate, and what’s included in the price. Target price range (best-effort, comps not provided): €170,000–€180,000 if finish is good but standard; hold closer to €185,000 only if truly top-tier fit-out + clean paperwork + quiet orientation.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern 3-room, 74 m² unit in a named complex, on a convenient 2nd floor, in a transit-accessible pocket with some parks nearby. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet (it’s on/near a major boulevard) or proven long-term build quality unless you verify the developer record, common areas, and association costs. If the finish is real and the unit is oriented away from traffic, this can be a very livable “plug-and-play” home.

#102
Apartament cu 4 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 4 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€200,000 · 4 rooms · Buiucani · пр-т Алба Юлия, 81/5, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper: renovated (“euro repair”), furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating + AC.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — huge duplex-style layout, 3 bathrooms, playground/video on site, good everyday amenities nearby. Investor ➖ — niche product (very large, 4-room, duplex); liquidity depends on building quality and true net area. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — “penthouse-ish” feel (top floors 5–6/6), privacy, entertaining-friendly living zone.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the size/features are compelling, but pricing hinges on verifying building type/quality, exact usable area, and duplex trade-offs (stairs, roof exposure).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €175,000–€195,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so confirm with 3–5 nearby sold/active listings for similar new-build duplexes). Ask for: proof of total vs usable sqm, year of construction + commissioning docs, HOA/maintenance costs, heating system details, parking terms (is it deeded or just “open”), and any roof/terrace waterproofing history. Use any ambiguity on “building type” and the fact it’s 5–6th floor duplex (stairs/heat load) as leverage.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, renovated, new-build, top-floor duplex in Buiucani with strong “liveability” (food/parks/groceries density) and modern comfort systems. You are NOT buying: a simple, highly liquid standard-family flat—this is a premium, idiosyncratic layout where documentation + build quality matter more than the ad copy. If docs check out and the building is solid, this can be a great long-term home; if not, it’s an expensive headache disguised as “euro repair.”

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€105,000 · 3 rooms · Rîșcani (Poșta Veche) · str-la Studenților, 11/1, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated), with the usual “check the workmanship + wiring/plumbing” caveat for older stock.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, separate kitchen, 2 bathrooms, balcony, playground nearby. Investor ➖ — rentable, but 4/5 without elevator risk + micro-area is more “everyday local” than premium. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — good daily convenience; less of the “walkable center vibe,” more residential practicality.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout for a family and good amenity density nearby, but you’re buying an older series building (and 4/5 stairs) so price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

List price is 105,000€ (~1,500€/m² if 70 m² is accurate). Without verified comps for this exact micro-area/building series, treat this as a “test-the-market” ask. Target price range: 95,000–102,000€ (aim low if staircase/building condition/no elevator, noise, or renovation quality shows weaknesses). Ask for: proof of renovation scope (electrics/plumbing), recent utility bills, roof condition (top floors matter), stairwell/entrance maintenance plan, parking reality ("open" often means first-come), and any moisture history.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 70 m² family apartment with good daily infrastructure density and a usable, classic layout. You are NOT buying: a premium “effortless resale” asset or an elevator building; stairs + older block economics come with it. If the renovation is truly solid and the stairwell/building is well-run, this can be a comfortable, low-drama home — at the right price.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” / unfinished shell — you’re buying a layout + views, not a finished home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (110 m², 2 bathrooms, high-floor light; parks/schools nearby) Investor: ➖ (large ticket + finishing capex; rentability depends on final finish + parking) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (panoramic glazing + 13/16 view, lots of food/drink within ~300 m)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (new build, size, floor heating, high floor) but unfinished status + agency listing means you must price in finishing cost and execution risk.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a full cost picture: developer handover spec, utility connections status, elevator/HOA readiness, and what “included” actually means (meters, boiler, floor heating manifold, windows spec). Target price range: €155,000–€165,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range based on unfinished condition + leverage from multiple similar units in the same building). If they won’t move on price, push for value: parking terms, finishing credit, or a clearly priced turnkey partner.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern 3-room canvas in Râșcani with panoramic potential and a very amenity-dense micro-area. You are NOT buying: certainty on final cost/timeline — finishing, coordination, and quality control are on you. If you hate projects, pass; if you want to customize and can manage contractors, this is a serious contender.

Buyer fit

Final verdict

Negotiation strategy

You are buying a large, modern, practical family apartment in a new residential complex with 2 baths and real separation of spaces. You are NOT buying a proven “walk-to-restaurants/transit” micro-area experience (at least per nearby POI signals). If the building checks out (management fees, soundproofing, elevator uptime), it’s a solid home — just don’t overpay on vibes.

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
