Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Telecentru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Telecentru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€89,500 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · str. Gheorghe Asachi, 11/1, Telecentru, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready, but it reads like a “live now, modernize later” cosmetic-renovation unit.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 2 balconies, 2nd floor in a 5-floor building is very livable. Investor ➖ — rentable, but Brezhnev-era stock + unknown condition of systems limits premium rent. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — good day-to-day convenience, but it’s not the most “boutique/quiet” vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong practical layout + autonomous heating, but this is older building stock and the ad is light on hard proof (photos/specs/engineering), so price needs to reflect risk.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for proof and use it as leverage: roof/risers status, heating paperwork, balcony condition, electrical upgrade, and any moisture history. Target price range (best-effort guess, comps not provided): €82,000–€87,000 if condition is truly cosmetic; €78,000–€82,000 if you find dated wiring/plumbing, weak windows, or balcony repairs.

Bottom line

You are buying a practical 3-room, mid-building ("middle" position) apartment in a very usable Telecentru pocket with lots of nearby daily-life amenities. You are NOT buying new-build comfort: expect stairwell/building-systems reality and budget for incremental upgrades. Autonomous heating is a real upside—verify it properly and price everything else accordingly.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€170,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Paris, 34/4, Chișinău, sectorul Buiucani
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“euro”), furnished + appliances, autonomous heating, AC, elevator, video/intercom.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground, parks nearby) Investor ➖ (good rentability in Buiucani, but higher capex already “priced in”) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (lots of food/drink nearby, walkable urban convenience)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready to live” package in a lively micro-area, but you should pressure-test price vs €/m² and building quality/management.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for the full cadastral extract + technical passport, confirm net vs gross 88 m², and verify developer/build year + HOA/maintenance + utility bills (autonomous heat can be great if the system is well maintained). Parking is “open” (not deeded), so don’t pay as if it’s guaranteed. Target price range: €155,000–€165,000 (best-effort; I don’t have verified comps for this exact street/building, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a newer-build, turnkey, higher-floor apartment with modern comforts and a very amenity-dense pocket. You are NOT buying: certainty on noise/traffic/views or a guaranteed private parking right (until documents and on-site reality confirm it).

#108
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€119,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Ion Neculce, 10, Buiucani, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white variant” shell: great blank canvas, but budget + time for full finishing.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, 2 loggias; calmer micro-area) Investor ➖ (saleability OK, but capex + finishing risk; size is good if layout is clean) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (quiet, green-leaning; not a restaurant/transit hotspot)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (75 m², mid-floor, warm, 2 baths) but you’re buying an unfinished apartment; price needs to compensate for finishing and unknowns.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a target price range of ~€105k–€112k (comps unknown here; adjust after you verify recent Buiucani 3-room sales + condition). Use leverage: unfinished “white variant” means immediate cash outlay; confirm elevator condition, building maintenance fees, and any major planned repairs. Request full docs (ownership, no encumbrances), and verify actual usable area vs total, balcony inclusions, and whether the 2nd bathroom is fully legal/connected.

Bottom line

You are buying: a warm, mid-building, mid-floor 75 m² 3-room with 2 bathrooms + 2 loggias in a quieter, greener Buiucani pocket. You are NOT buying: a turnkey home or a lively walk-out-the-door café/transit lifestyle (the immediate 300m amenity signal looks park-heavy, light on transit/food). If your finish budget is tight, this listing can turn into “cheap on paper, expensive in reality.”

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: described as renovated, furnished, with appliances, autonomous heating + AC.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 separate rooms, 103 m², 2 loggias, playground + elevator. Investor ➖ — new-build and “ready to rent”, but price point likely narrows tenant pool. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — boulevard view + strong daily convenience (groceries/food/transit density).

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “turnkey” package in a convenient Ciocana micro-area, but the ask looks premium and needs justification via comps and building quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €215k–€230k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so validate vs recent 999.md deals for similar 3-room, ~100 m², AMIC/new-build, mid-floor on Mircea cel Bătrân). Ask for: exact year of completion, HOA/maintenance, utility bills (winter), proof/details of “Euroreparație” scope, parking terms (is it deeded/assigned or just open), and noise mitigation (boulevard-facing).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, turnkey 3-room with practical extras (office/cabinet, 2 loggias, elevator, autonomous heat). You are NOT buying: quiet/courtyard serenity—boulevard exposure likely means noise/dust and a view premium you should not overpay for. If build quality + management check out, it’s a solid live-in option; if not, treat it as a convenience buy, not a “forever” asset.

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great layout/size and a modern complex, but the 10/10 top-floor premium and unknown building/maintenance quality mean you should press hard on price and documents.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235k–€250k (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted ask based on premium finish + top-floor factors). Push on: proof of 117 m² in cadastral docs, condo/maintenance fees, elevator/service history, roof/waterproofing, heating system age, and what exactly stays (inventory list). Use “agency listing + 0% commission” to argue they already have margin.

Bottom line

You are buying: space (117 m²) + a practical family plan (3 bedrooms, 2 baths) in a modern, amenity-rich compound. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/comfort on the top floor unless roof/elevator/building management are solid. At this price, you want boring paperwork, boring systems, and zero surprises — otherwise you’re overpaying for “shiny.”

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “- Kaufland”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 2 grocery shops, 5 food/drink places, 6 parks/playgrounds, and 2 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Rîșcani is described as being in the northern part of Chișinău (with historical context for the former village/area).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

#112
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€219,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Pan Halippa, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: “euro” finish, furnished, autonomous heating, and two bathrooms suggest a turnkey setup (verify actual condition on viewing).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 bedrooms + 2 baths + schools/kindergarten nearby) Investor ➖ (premium central pricing may cap yield; works better as high-end long-term rental than pure yield play) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Centru, lots of food/parks/transit; elevator + parking options)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — Strong central location + new-build stack + 82 m², but floor 10/13 and “agency premium” pricing mean you should push hard on price and proof (docs, view, noise).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €195k–€210k (comps unknown, so this is a risk-adjusted bid range, not a comp-backed valuation). Ask for: exact legal area breakdown, building handover year, monthly maintenance, parking terms (is a space included or just “available?”), heating system papers, and a written inventory list for furniture/appliances. Use floor 10 + potential boulevard noise/wind + any elevator/parking fees as leverage; if they won’t move on price, negotiate parking inclusion, notary/registration costs, and a proper defect checklist.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, central, family-sized apartment with high convenience (elevator, autonomous heating, two baths) and a dense, walkable micro-area. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet, guaranteed view permanence, or a “cheap” deal—this should be treated as a premium lifestyle purchase unless the price drops. If the building/management is solid and the interior is truly high quality, it’s a good Centru play; if not, the premium evaporates fast.

€117,824 · 4 rooms · Râșcani · str. Calea Moșilor, 4, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely close to move-in ready (new-build “Smart Home” complex), but final depends on what finish level the developer delivers (shell vs turnkey).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 4 rooms + 2 bathrooms + nearby school/kindergarten claims; 9/14 keeps you above street noise. Investor ➖ — 4-room resale/rent pool is thinner; works better as “premium family rental” than as high-yield micro-unit play. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — building amenities help, but immediate 300m vibe looks a bit thin (few cafes/parks right next door).

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong on layout + new-build positioning, but you’re paying for “premium” while the micro-area amenities (parks/food) look limited in the immediate walk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €108,000–€115,000 (comps unknown, so this is a risk-adjusted ask). Push on: (1) exact finish package, (2) smart-home scope (what’s actually included), (3) parking price/availability, (4) maintenance fees, (5) warranty/hand-over date. If it’s not turnkey, price the finishing budget explicitly and subtract it.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, family-sized unit in a new-build complex with modern positioning and a practical 2-bath layout. You are NOT buying: a proven “walk-outside-into-busy-streets” location—this reads more residential/quiet than lively. Watch-outs: developer promises + delivery specs matter more than the marketing here; verify paperwork and what’s included in the price.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€420,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · strada București 26, Centru, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: “white variant” shell means you’ll likely need full finishes + kitchen + lighting to move in.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (space + 2 baths + terrace; playground courtyard is a real quality-of-life perk) Investor ➖ (premium product + small building = niche resale/rent pool; works only if you’re targeting high-end tenants) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (club-house vibe, concierge, terrace, high ceilings)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — looks like a genuinely premium micro-building in central Chișinău, but the “developer shell” finish + headline luxury positioning means you should push hard on total cost-to-move-in and price-per-m².

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €380k–€405k (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Make the seller quantify what’s included vs. not included (finishing scope, terrace waterproofing spec, HVAC/boiler brand, underfloor zones, parking terms). Use (1) white-variant finishing cost, (2) terrace risk/maintenance, and (3) “free parking” being non-deeded/less bankable as leverage. If they won’t move on price, ask for upgrades/finish credit or guaranteed parking allocation in writing.

Bottom line

You are buying: a low-density, security/concierge, high-ceiling central home with a real terrace (rare) and a calm courtyard. You are NOT buying: turnkey luxury at €420k — you’re still funding and managing the finish-out. Also: the immediate area reads more “central + green pockets + transit” than “restaurant strip,” so expect convenient, not buzzing-nightlife-on-your-doorstep.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: it’s a new-build “white-box” finish (you still need a full interior fit-out), but the core perks (autonomous heating, warm floors in key zones, elevator, intercom) set you up for a clean, modern build.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 92 m², 3 bedrooms + wardrobe, 2 bathrooms, high floor; good day-to-day layout. Investor ➖ — strong complex + demand area, but white-box means capex + time; frontage to main road can be a tenant filter. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — panoramic glazing and amenities are nice, but road-facing orientation trades “buzz” for noise/air quality risk.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great layout and complex, but you’re paying for the brand/new-build + you must budget the full finishing; road-facing windows should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112,000–€118,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have true local comps here). Push on (1) white-box finishing cost + time, (2) main-road exposure (noise/dust), (3) any paid extras unclear in the ad (parking, balcony/veranda finishing, developer handover specifics). If they won’t move on headline price, negotiate “value”: included parking, upgraded glazing/ventilation solutions, or staged payments with a discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, family-sized, new-build 3BR with 2 baths in a branded Telecentru complex with strong on-site infrastructure. You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment or a quiet micro-location — this is likely a “close the windows at rush hour” address unless the glazing is excellent. A good deal if you price the fit-out honestly and you’re fine trading serenity for convenience + newer construction.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (109 m², 2 bathrooms, balcony, floor 4/12 is practical) Investor ➖ (size is great, but “finish it yourself” ties up cash/time; rent-ready timeline uncertain) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (panoramic glazing + services nearby; good city convenience)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (new build, brick, autonomous heating, 109 m², mid-floor), but the finishing budget and execution risk should come straight off the price.

Negotiation strategy

Bottom line

You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment — your final cost depends on finishing scope, contractors, and how the building is run. Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: euro-renovated, furnished, appliances included; elevator building helps, but duplex top floors add day-to-day friction.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 5 rooms + 2 bathrooms + playground nearby; good for “separate spaces” living. Investor ➖ — could rent to a larger household, but duplex + top-floor premiums narrow the tenant pool. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — Buiucani with strong walkable amenities; panoramic windows can feel great if the view/noise works.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (100 m², 5 rooms, renovated, furnished), but 9th/10th-floor duplex risk (heat, roof/elevator dependence, resale audience) should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €95,000–€102,000 (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted bid). Anchor on: top-floor duplex maintenance/comfort risk, any elevator downtime, and confirm exact legal area + room layout. Ask for: utility bills (summer/winter), roof history, elevator service records, and any formalization for the “2 levels” configuration.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, ready-to-live family apartment in a lively, amenity-dense Buiucani pocket. You are NOT buying: a low-maintenance, universally liquid resale — duplex top floors are a love-it-or-hate-it segment. If the light/view is excellent and winter bills are sane, it’s a strong value; if not, walk or reprice hard.

The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“eurorepair”), furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating + double-glazed windows.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, floor 2/5, parks + schools mentioned, playground nearby)
Investor ➖ (rentable, but unknown building year/condition and no sqm/price-per-sqm context beyond 70 m² claim)
Lifestyle buyer ➖ (Buiucani is pleasant/residential; restaurant density looks modest in the 300m snapshot)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package in a livable Buiucani micro-area, but you should price-check hard because the listing reads like agency marketing and we lack building/renovation quality proof.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €115,000–€121,000 (best-effort guess; comps unknown). Use (1) proof of renovation quality (wiring/plumbing, insulation, boiler docs), (2) building condition (roof/stairwell/pipe risers), and (3) furniture/appliance age as leverage. Ask what’s actually included in the sale and get it written. If any humidity/ventilation issues appear (common in older 5-floor stock), re-price immediately.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-leaning 3-room layout with 2 baths on a convenient mid-low floor, in a green, non-touristy pocket with daily needs nearby.
You are NOT buying: a proven “investment” without comp support, nor guaranteed renovation quality—verify everything that’s hidden behind fresh finishes.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build delivery (2025) in “white box” finish means you’re buying a promise + a fit-out project.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, secure courtyard + playground, elevator, optional parking. Investor ➖ — good “new build” appeal, but micro-location looks thin on cafés/parks right nearby; returns depend on tenant profile + final delivery quality. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — building perks are strong, but walk-out daily vibe seems quiet (few nearby food/parks signals).

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec package (brick, insulation, REHAU, OTIS, courtyard/parking) but you’re taking delivery/finish risk and the immediate amenity density looks modest.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a developer-style deal: either price relief or value-add (parking discount, storage included, staged payments, or finishing credit). Target price range: €115,000–€120,000 (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted target vs the €122,840 ask at ~€1,550/m²).

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, security-forward new-build 3-room with solid “spec-sheet” comforts (autonomous gas heating, good windows, sound isolation, elevator, courtyard). You are NOT buying: immediate street-level bustle or proven building track record until delivery; you’ll still need budget/time for finishing and snagging.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + playground + elevator; good day-to-day convenience) Investor ➖ (new-build supply + developer pricing can cap upside; depends on rental demand near Port Mall) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (walkable amenities density; easier “city living” without being in the core)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong micro-location for Ciocana + good feature set, but developer-sale pricing and unknown unit specifics (views/sun/noise/finish quality) mean you should push terms.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112k–€118k (comps unknown from the provided data; use this as an opening band, then tighten after checking same-complex resales and delivered-quality reviews). Ask for: (1) upgraded windows/door package confirmation, (2) a written spec sheet for sound insulation, (3) parking/storage bundle discount, (4) staged payments or a cash discount vs the 4-year installments, (5) clear delivery/penalty terms.

Bottom line

You are buying a new-build 3-room in a convenience-heavy node (near a major mall + transit + daily services). You are NOT buying instant move-in — the “white box” finish shifts cost and risk onto you. If the unit faces a busy boulevard, you’re also buying traffic noise risk; verify orientation and insulation in writing.

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” finish means you’re buying a shell you must complete (kitchen/baths/finishes).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (82.4 sqm, 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, playground + open parking) Investor ➖ (new build helps, but micro-amenities near the building look thin; rentability depends on exact transit access) Lifestyle buyer ❌ (this reads more “practical residential” than walkable/cafe-heavy)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout in a new block, but the area signals “car/transit dependent” and you still have a full finishing budget to swallow.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a discount explicitly tied to (1) finishing costs for a white-variant unit and (2) any building/area downsides (noise from Vadul lui Vodă corridor, parking reality, courtyard quality). Target price range: €102,000–€108,000 (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted ask, not a proven market comp). If they won’t move on price, push for seller/agency to cover part of finishing (or include materials/contractor package) and confirm all utilities + autonomous heating documentation.

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build 3-room with sane family layout (2 baths) on a low floor (2/9) that you can finish “your way.” You are NOT buying: a turn-key home or a naturally walkable, amenity-dense neighborhood pocket right outside the entrance. If you’re okay managing renovations and you rely on car/transit anyway, this can be a solid value play at the right price.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances + autonomous heating), but you still want a hard check on wiring/plumbing and any “fresh paint” cover-ups.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 4 rooms / 95 m² + schools/kindergarten nearby + playground + two balconies. Investor ➖ — rentable, but Ciocana demand is more price-sensitive; yield hinges on true condition and building maintenance. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — decent daily convenience (groceries/parks), but not a “walkable center” vibe.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good size and practical layout for a family, but it’s 5/9 in secondary stock and the listing language feels “agency-polished,” so price should reflect any building/HOA and renovation quality risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €130,000–€138,000 (best-effort guess; no comps provided, so treat as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Ask for utility bills (winter) and any renovation receipts.

Bottom line

You are buying space and a functional family setup (95 m², individual partitioning, two balconies) in a well-served daily-life micro-area. You are NOT buying a prime-central, nightlife-on-foot neighbourhood — it’s more practical than charming. If the building is well-run and the renovation is real (not cosmetic), this can be a solid “live in for years” pick at the right price.

Move-in ready on paper: renovated, furnished, equipped, autonomous heating, elevator building.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 120 m² + 2 bathrooms + terrace + nearby schools/parks; floor 9 can be great if you like light and quiet. Investor ➖ — premium ticket size; works only if you believe in long-term prime-center demand and building quality. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — Central boulevard energy, walk-to-cafés, high-floor views, “lock-and-leave” convenience.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong central location + big size, but the price is “top shelf”; you’re buying the building quality and layout as much as the address.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: exact building year/developer, maintenance fee, winter heating bills, sound insulation, parking rights (assigned vs. first-come), terrace waterproofing docs, and a daylight/video walkthrough (including elevator lobby + stairwell). Target price range: €395k–€420k (best-effort without comps; if the building is truly high-quality new construction and the terrace is substantial, the seller may hold closer to ask).

Bottom line

You are buying a large, renovated, high-floor center apartment with amenities and daily convenience. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet (center) or guaranteed parking certainty (open parking can be chaotic). If the building/developer checks out and the terrace is proven leak-free, this is a “pay-up-for-comfort” kind of deal.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Vă propunem spre vânzare acest apartament cu 3 camere, sect. Centru, str. București, cu suprafața totală de 120 mp.”.

Additional stated detail: “Compartimentat în: 3 dormitoare, bucătărie, 2 blocuri sanitare, terasă, antreu.”. Also noted: “* Încălzire autonomă;”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

(Structured field) Panels (999.md): Balcon/ lojie, Compartimentare, Etaj, Euroreparație, Fond locativ, Grup sanitar, Loc de parcare, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Starea apartamentului, Suprafață totală. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 3 grocery shops, 8 food/drink places, 21 parks/playgrounds, and 10 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Centru.

Street (from listing fields): București.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~28.3/km², grocery density ~10.6/km², transit-stop density ~35.4/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Centru

€91,110 · 3 rooms · Durlești, Centru · Durlești, Centru, str. Cartușa
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Not move-in ready: “white variant” finish in a new-build delivery timeline (2026) means you’re buying a shell that still needs budget + decisions.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 86 m², 2 bathrooms, terrace + nearby schools/parks reads like daily-life friendly (if commute works). Investor ➖ — delivery risk + finish costs; rentability depends on demand for Durlești and how “penthouse” actually translates on site. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — top-floor terrace + autonomous heating + elevator is a strong comfort package.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive layout/size for the money, but you’re taking execution risk (2026 delivery) and paying for a marketing-heavy listing.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82,000–€87,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps here). Push on (1) “penthouse” label vs actual terrace usability/exposure, (2) white-variant finish cost credit, (3) delivery date risk, (4) parking specifics (is it truly included or just “available”). If they won’t move on price, ask for upgrades (door/intercom/AC prep) or a written finish package.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, new-build 3-room-with-living concept (marketed as penthouse) with 2 baths, terrace, elevator, autonomous heating. You are NOT buying: a turnkey home or a proven micro-location with lots of immediate cafes/food options right outside the door. You’re betting that Durlești convenience + parks + transit carry the lifestyle while the building quality holds up after handover.

Ad · Maps · Street View

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build, “euro renovation,” furnished + appliances, autonomous heat + underfloor heating.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 bedrooms + 2 bathrooms, safe courtyard/playground, on-site shops) Investor ➖ (new-build demand is good, but price-per-m² risk and 11th-floor liquidity depends on elevator/build quality) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (high floor light/views, modern finishes, walk-to-essentials density)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong turnkey package in a convenient micro-area, but the premium price needs to be justified by building quality, HOA fees, and total “all-in” costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €160k–€170k (comps unknown; treat as an opening band, not a valuation). Use (1) agency listing, (2) high floor (11/12), (3) verify actual furniture/appliances included, (4) any snag-list defects, (5) monthly maintenance + parking terms to push for a discount or included underground parking/storage.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, efficient 65 m² 2BR+living in a gated new-build with strong day-to-day convenience. You are NOT buying a “cheap m²” deal—this is paying for finish level + new-build narrative, so due diligence is the whole game. High floor is a feature if the building systems are solid; it’s a headache if elevators/management are weak.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: recent “Euro” renovation, furnished, with appliances; typical snag list risk only (finish quality, ventilation, plumbing fittings).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 bedrooms + living, balcony, playground; 3/7 is stroller-friendly) Investor ➖ (easy to rent if priced right, but Durlești resale/rent demand can be more price-sensitive) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (lots of nearby cafes/amenities; more “busy-local” than postcard-central)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package for 65 m², but Durlești pricing can drift; make the price prove itself with docs + build quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105,000–€112,000 (best-effort; comps not provided). Push on (a) developer/build quality, (b) any missing/unclear docs, (c) what “club-type house” actually means operationally (fees, rules), (d) furniture/appliances valuation (don’t overpay for used items). Ask for: commissioning/acceptance paperwork, building management details, last utility bills (autonomous heating costs), and proof the layout is legally reflected.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, mid-floor, furnished 2+living layout in a convenience-heavy micro-area. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/green “old-center” vibes or a comp-validated market bargain unless the seller meets you on price. The upside: liquidity via move-in readiness; the risk: paying “Centru” money for Durlești if the surroundings/build don’t match the marketing.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: “Euro renovation”, furnished, appliances installed, autonomous heating + underfloor heating suggests a turnkey spec.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground + closed courtyard are family-coded. Investor ➖ — rentable, but 4-room/71 m² is a narrower tenant pool and cap rates may be softer at this ticket. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — Buiucani with solid daily amenities + quick transit, plus balcony and security.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong package (new build, security, parking potential, turnkey finish), but price is the whole story: without confirmed sqm/condition proof beyond broker text and without comps, you should push for value or extras.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €170k–€178k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). If seller won’t move, negotiate “value add”: include a parking spot / storage, confirm high-quality materials with invoices, and require a clean technical/ownership file. Use the “71 m² for 4 rooms” angle: tighter room sizes can be a future resale objection.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, guarded, amenity-rich new-build setup in Buiucani that’s optimized for day-to-day living. You are NOT buying generous room proportions; this is an efficiency plan where layout quality matters more than raw square meters. If the building is truly well-managed (24/7 security, closed yard, functioning elevators), this can be a very comfortable long-term base — but don’t overpay without comp support.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€154,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Vasile Lupu
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white-box” finish in a new-build—structure is done, but you still have to pay/time-manage full interior finishing.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (2 bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground/green nearby) Investor ➖ (rentable, but 9th floor + finishing capex; yields depend heavily on your all-in cost) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (new building, security/cameras, good transit, low tourist noise)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — Good Buiucani new-build fundamentals (78 m², 2 baths, autonomous heating, security) but you’re paying “new-build premium” while still funding the finish.

Negotiation strategy

Ask the agent to justify price with comps for similar 78 m² units in Buiucani, delivered/finished vs white-box; comps are unknown from the provided data. Current ask implies ~€1,974/m². Best-effort target range: €140k–€150k (lower end if you find any finishing/quality uncertainties; higher end only if building reputation + views + layout are genuinely top-tier). Make the finish-cost explicit: request a discount or credits tied to a realistic finishing budget and timeline.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, security-managed building in a generally livable/green-feeling micro-area with solid daily amenities. You are NOT buying turnkey convenience—this is a project (finishes, fixtures, coordination) plus the risks that come with new-build execution quality. 9th floor is great for light/views, but only if elevators/building management are reliable day-to-day.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În imediată apropiere se găsesc numeroase puncte sociale și comerciale, supermarket, farmacie, bancă, grădiniță, sală de fitness, parc etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

(Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Close to move-in ready (new-build shell/finish level is the big unknown; the building amenities look “done”).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (big area, 2 bedrooms + multiple baths, terrace) Investor ➖ (3 baths + penthouse layout is lifestyle-driven; rentability depends on finishes and elevator/building reputation) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (penthouse feel, terrace/loggia, panoramic windows, parking, elevator)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong space/value-per-sqm on paper for a new-build penthouse, but the micro-location looks “quiet/residential” (low nearby dining) and the finish/hand-over specifics will decide if it’s a steal or a headache.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for (1) exact handover condition (white box vs fully finished), (2) what’s included (kitchen, doors, bathrooms), (3) heating/utility costs + management fee, (4) terrace waterproofing warranty, (5) parking price included or separate. Target price range (best-effort guess, comps unknown): €128k–€138k if parking is included and finishes are decent; if it’s unfinished or parking is extra, push lower or demand developer concessions (fit-out credit / parking discount / extended warranty).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, new-build, top-floor lifestyle apartment with terrace/loggia and “modern-complex” perks (elevator, video, underground parking). You are NOT buying: a plug-and-play central, restaurant-rich neighborhood vibe—this reads more like parks + basics + transit, not an evening street-life hub.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (finished to a high spec and sold furnished/appliances).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 91 m², 2 bathrooms + 2 balconies, closed courtyard + playground, mid-low floor. Investor ➖ — premium price tier; likely good tenant demand, but yield may be thin unless you buy well. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — modern building, quiet elevators, parking option, decent walkability for daily errands.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “new-build + full fit-out” package, but at this price in Ciocana you’re paying for finish/complex; make the numbers and building ops bulletproof.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps unknown): €235,000–€245,000. Levers: (1) require proof/clarity on what exactly stays (furniture list + brands/models), (2) confirm underground parking is included vs optional and price it separately, (3) check monthly building fees + sinking fund + management quality, (4) verify sound/thermal insulation claims with an on-site “quiet test” (neighbors, corridor, elevator shaft), (5) use any cosmetic/maintenance notes from viewing to justify a trim.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, family-friendly, security-heavy new-build in a practical, amenity-dense pocket. You are NOT buying a central, nightlife-first location—this is convenience and building quality over prestige. If parking isn’t included, treat that as a real cost and renegotiate the headline price accordingly.

Buyer fit

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid spec on paper (newer build, 90 m², 2 baths, 8/12), but the immediate walkable amenity signal is weak and price needs to reflect that.

Negotiation strategy

Bottom line

You are buying a large, modern-ish, family-friendly apartment with a practical layout and low friction move-in. You are NOT buying a walk-out-the-door urban lifestyle; expect some car dependency and do a commute/noise reality check. If the parking and operating costs don’t check out, this is an easy price haircut, not a deal-breaker.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “-in apropiere aveti tot necesarul pentru a duce un mod de viata sanatos si frumos si anume: restaurante, spa, sala de sport, fitness, farmacii, magazine, cafenele, scoli si deasemenea privelistea nemaipomenit de frumo…”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 2 grocery shops, 18 food/drink places, 2 parks/playgrounds, and 2 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Decebal is described as being in Botanica, about 3 km long, running from Piața Iulius Cezar to the intersection with bd. Dacia. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Decebal din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

€99,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Chișinău, Botanica, Костюженская, 8/5
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready, but “cosmetic renovation” usually means you’ll want a budget for paint/lighting/bath refresh to make it feel modern.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 70 m², 2 balconies, parks nearby; good everyday layout potential. Investor ➖ — panel block + micro-area seems light on transit/POIs; rent demand may be steady but not “premium”. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — green surroundings, but not a cafe/walk-to-everything pocket based on nearby counts.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional package (70 m², 5/9 with elevator, balconies, furnished) but panel building + “cosmetic” finish caps the ceiling at this price.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on the building type (panel), condition (cosmetic), and the micro-area signals (low transit/food nearby in the 300m snapshot). Ask for: (1) building maintenance/roof/elevator status, (2) winter heating costs, (3) any moisture on exterior panel joints, (4) proof of legal status/ownership chain. Target price range: €90,000–€96,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified local comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, mid-floor, family-sized unit in a classic 9-floor panel block with elevator and outdoor space. You are NOT buying: a high-end finish or a high-amenity, transit-rich micro-location—expect some trade-offs in “walk-to-life” convenience. If the building is well-managed and the interior is truly “ready”, it’s a good value at the right number; if not, push hard on price or pass.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: fresh “turnkey” renovation, furnished, appliances, AC, new windows/doors mentioned.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 2 separate bedrooms + kitchen/living layout + playground + parks nearby. Investor ➖ — good rentability, but price-per-m² vs local comps needs checking; agency listing. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — lots of food/drink spots and parks in close walking range; “quiet courtyard” claim fits a calmer vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong micro-location and practical 3-room layout, but you should pressure-test renovation quality, building condition, and price vs comps.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €108,000–€115,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified local comps here). Use: (1) agency/marketing “exclusive” language to push back, (2) verification points (electrics/plumbing scope, permits, insulation, warranty), (3) building realities (3/5 walk-up, secondary market, shared systems) to justify a haircut.

Bottom line

You are buying a functional 72 m², 3-room, renovated “don’t-think-just-move” apartment in a busy, amenity-rich Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying certainty on build/renovation quality until you see documents, meter/pipework, and a serious inspection. If the renovation is cosmetic-only, this becomes a price problem fast—negotiate like it’s your job.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + terrace + park-facing; needs finishing effort) Investor ➖ (strong location view premium, but 200k + fit-out narrows tenant/buyer pool) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Dendrarium-facing terrace is the whole point)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the view/park adjacency is top-tier, but you must price in white-box completion and verify build/HOA realities.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; no comps provided): €180k–€192k, or keep €200k only if the terrace/view is truly unobstructed + building quality checks out. Push on (1) white-box completion cost, (2) any developer/commission padding, (3) noise/traffic exposure on Constantin Stere, and (4) documentation/commissioning timeline.

Bottom line

You are buying a new-build, park-facing lifestyle asset with a “rare” terrace angle. You are NOT buying turnkey comfort—expect a full finishing project and all the usual new-build unknowns. If the view is real and stable, it’s a keeper; if it’s compromised, the premium evaporates fast.

Buyer fit

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid practical unit in a modern block, but price-per-m² risk is high because it’s only 65 m² for a “4-room” and the immediate amenity density looks thin.

Negotiation strategy

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, low-friction family layout (2nd floor, autonomous heat, finished, furnished) in a green-feeling pocket. You are NOT buying a big 4-room by space standards — you’re buying smart partitioning inside 65 m². If you care about “step outside into cafés + groceries”, this micro-spot may feel car-dependent unless the data snapshot is missing key POIs.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper for a new-build: autonomous heating + insulated windows + elevator; verify actual finish level, noise insulation, and handover timeline.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 2 bedrooms + 2 baths + school/kindergarten pipeline + playground/elevator is a strong daily-life setup. Investor ➖ — good unit spec, but “developer / multiple floors available / 30% down” hints at lots of similar supply; rents depend on real transit/commute pull. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — plenty of nearby parks and lots of food/coffee density; expect a more “residential + complex life” vibe than old-town charm.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive functional layout (82 m², 2 baths, veranda) and new-build perks, but new-complex inventory and pricing leverage should exist.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €98,000–€103,000 (comps unknown; using leverage from developer inventory + multi-floor availability). Push for either a direct discount or value-adds (parking/storage, finished walls/doors/sanitary, appliance package, or notary/registration fees covered). Confirm monthly maintenance and any “complex” fees, plus heating system brand/warranty.

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build, family-friendly 3-room layout with 2 baths and modern infrastructure around it. You are NOT buying: scarcity or a unique unit—this reads like a standardized developer product where terms matter as much as the sticker price. Check hard before committing: final finishing scope, elevator/parking reality, soundproofing, and whether the veranda is legally counted/usable year-round.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (quality renovation + autonomous heating + underfloor heat), but confirm build quality + noise/wind exposure at floor 9.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (big 152 m², 2 bathrooms, playground, parks nearby) Investor ➖ (high ticket; rental demand exists but yield likely not amazing at this price) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (panoramic view + good transit; fewer “walk-out-the-door” restaurants)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great size/spec in a new-build stack, but Botanica + high floor + “agency polish” pricing means you should buy only if the building, management, and parking are truly strong.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring at 335k–345k and try to land 350k–360k (comps not provided, so this is a leverage-based range, not a comp-based valuation). Ask to itemize what’s included (parking spots, furniture/appliances, storage) and use any gaps (no included parking, weak HOA, poor soundproofing, low-quality finishes) to push price down.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern-feeling family apartment with comfort features (autonomous heating + underfloor heat) and a high-floor view. You are NOT buying: a restaurant-heavy “step outside and stroll” micro-neighborhood; you’ll likely be commuting to the fun. At €370k, the building’s execution (elevators, insulation, management, parking reality) matters more than the staging.

The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €370 000. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Etaj, Fond locativ, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Suprafață locativă, Suprafață totală. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 3 grocery shops, 4 food/drink places, 7 parks/playgrounds, and 9 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

#141
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€103,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica, Dacia · Chișinău, Botanica, Dacia, 38
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: likely livable now (cosmetic repair), but budget for refresh + systems check.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + 63 m² + higher floor can work well if layout is sane. Investor ➖ — rentability OK, but price per m² may be tight unless condition/location is stronger than it reads. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — practical, transit-heavy micro-area; not a “cute walk-everywhere” vibe.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — 7/9 floor and workable size are solid, but “cosmetic repair” often hides dated electrics/plumbing/finishes; price needs to reflect that.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €92,000–€100,000 (comps unknown; adjust after you confirm building type/age, elevator condition, and actual renovation quality). Push on: cosmetic-only renovation, any old windows/doors, noisy boulevard exposure, elevator reliability, and proof of paperwork readiness.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 3-room in Botanica/Dacia with strong transit access and everyday services nearby. You are NOT buying: a turnkey “done” apartment or a quiet boutique micro-neighbourhood—expect trade-offs (noise, common areas, finishing quality).

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€95,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Grenoble 163/1, sectorul Botanica, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: cosmetic-renovation condition, but already equipped (furniture + appliances) with autonomous heating and AC.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 70 m², 2 bathrooms, 3 balconies; practical layout for daily life. Investor ➖ — rentable, but panel block + cosmetic finish means tenant profile is “value” not premium. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — strong green-space vibe nearby, but this is more functional-residential than buzz/central.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong utility (70 m², 2 baths, autonomous heat, 8/10 with elevator), but panel building + “cosmetic” finish caps the upside at €95k.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €85,000–€90,000 (comps not provided, so this is a best-effort value stance). Use anchors: panel construction, cosmetic renovation (future refresh budget), floor 8/10 (great view but resale pool narrower), and verify: autonomy paperwork for heating, elevator condition/fees, roof/insulation issues typical for panel blocks, and any balcony legalization/alterations.

Bottom line

You are buying space efficiency: 3 rooms + 2 bathrooms + multiple balconies in a practical residential pocket. You are NOT buying a “premium building” story — panel block and cosmetic finish mean price discipline matters. If documents/maintenance check out, it’s a solid live-in unit with good day-to-day comfort (autonomous heat + parking + security).

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build delivery expected end of this year; “white box” finish means you still need full fit-out budget + time.

Buyer fit

Family ➖ (good size + 2 bathrooms, but 9/18 + construction phase risk) Investor ✔ (91 m², new-build, structured installment plan; likely solid rental demand in Botanica if pricing holds) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (panoramic view upside; immediate walkable cafe/park vibe seems limited in the 300m micro-radius)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive new-build spec (brick, insulation, underground parking, view), but delivery/finish risk + unclear total all-in cost (repairs, furnishing, closing) means price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €128,000–€135,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a risk-adjusted ask rather than a comp-anchored number). Push on: end-of-year handover uncertainty, “white box” finishing costs, and any developer/complex fees. If you want the installment plan, negotiate not only price but also payment schedule flexibility (later/softer milestones, penalty terms, and early repayment discount).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room in a modern high-rise with view potential, solid building materials, and parking. You are NOT buying: turnkey living — expect finishing works, possible delays/defects punch-list, and a micro-area that may feel residential/quiet rather than café-dense right outside the door.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€199,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Ghica Vodă 5/2, Botanica, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new renovation, sold furnished/equipped; low likelihood of immediate capex.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 75 m², 3 bedrooms, floor 2/10, courtyard playground + lots of nearby green space. Investor ➖ — easy to rent (new build, turnkey), but price-per-m² likely premium for Botanica; yields may be tight. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — quieter, residential feel; some nearby cafés/groceries, but not a “buzzing center” micro-area.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong turnkey package in a newer building, but €199k for 75 m² in Botanica needs a value check vs. recent comps and exact building quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €180k–€190k (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified local comps here). Push points: (1) agency listing + “fully furnished” often bakes in margin; (2) combined bathroom limits family usability; (3) verify what “warm floor” covers (whole flat or only wet zones) + boiler/service history; (4) confirm noise/exposure (panoramic windows can mean road-facing) and insulation. Ask for: building commissioning docs, maintenance fee, utility history (winter gas/electric), and exact furniture/tech inventory included.

Bottom line

You are buying a turnkey, modern, family-oriented apartment in a newer block with easy daily-life infrastructure. You are NOT buying “central nightlife/walk-all-evening” energy; this is more practical comfort than city-core vibe. At this price, the only smart move is to verify finishes + building reputation, then negotiate hard unless comps prove it’s fair.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” (unfinished) means you’re buying a clean shell with autonomy (good), but you’ll still spend real time/money to finish.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, balcony/terrace, playground + schools/parks nearby reads very family-forward. Investor ➖ — could rent well if finish quality is strong, but suburb liquidity + fit-out capex + agency listing hype add risk. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — you’ll get space + air + amenities, but it won’t feel like true city-center walk-out living.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and new-build basics, but it’s unfinished and the “Centru” labeling within Stăuceni is marketing; price should reflect fit-out and resale friction.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105k–€115k (best-effort guess; I don’t have direct comps). Anchor on unfinished condition (“white variant”), unknown exact finish scope, and confirmables (building quality, elevator performance for 9/10 floor, noise/parking). If seller resists, push for seller-paid finishing items or a documented materials/works budget credit.

Bottom line

You are buying a new-build 3-room family layout with autonomous heating, 2 bathrooms, outdoor space, and lots of nearby day-to-day amenities. You are NOT buying turnkey, and you’re not buying the liquidity/premium of a true Chișinău central address. If the building is solid, this is a good “space per euro” play — but only at the right number after you price the finishing.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, autonomous heating) — main diligence is “apartment in a private 3-level house” specifics.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (space + quiet/green, parking) Investor ➖ (area seems low on cafes/footfall; resale liquidity depends on legal/ownership structure) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (green/quiet yes; walk-to-bars/restaurants no)

Final verdict

Negotiation strategy

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room, house-like setup with autonomy (separate entrance vibe) and greenery. You are NOT buying: central walkability, restaurant density, or “easy-to-sell to anyone” liquidity. If you want calm + space and you drive, it can be a great live-in pick — at the right price.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on finishes (designer renovation); make sure the “good investment” claim is backed by invoices, photos of rough-in, and a snag list.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (95 m² + 3 rooms + higher floor = space + quieter) Investor ➖ (price is premium; rental demand depends on building/parking/management fees) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (newer brick building, views/light at 12/15, solid day-to-day amenities)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “done” interior in a newer building, but at this price you must verify build quality, monthly fees, and resale liquidity in Ciocana.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €230k–€245k (best-effort; I don’t have reliable comps here). Anchor on: (1) developer/management reputation + any known issues, (2) elevator/roof/water pressure performance at high floors, (3) parking/legal status, (4) proof of renovation scope (electrical/plumbing), (5) any mismatch between “3 rooms + living” wording and legal plan.

Bottom line

You are buying: a spacious, newly-built, high-floor 3-room home with a premium renovation and decent everyday convenience nearby. You are NOT buying: guaranteed top-tier central lifestyle or automatic investment yield at this ticket price—this needs due diligence and price discipline. You’re paying for finishes; make sure the building fundamentals (maintenance, elevators, noise, heat/water) deserve the premium.

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Vind apartament cu 3 camere spatios reparatie executata cu designer, apartametul nu a fost facut pentru vinzare si sa investit bine in el”.

(Structured field) Panels (999.md): Dezvoltator, Etaj, Euroreparație, Fond locativ, Living, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Starea apartamentului, Suprafață locativă, Suprafață totală, Tip clădire. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 11 food/drink places, 101 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances, autonomous heating) — assume you’ll still want a snagging check on a 10th-floor unit.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, close to schools/parks, playground) Investor: ➖ (works if rental demand is strong on this street; price needs to be tight) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Centru address, elevator, parking, easy transit)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/features are strong, but you should push price because local “walk-out-to-bars” signal looks modest and we don’t have verified comps.

Negotiation strategy

Start with a clean, rational offer anchored on missing/uncertain bits (view/noise, building management, real net area vs. 82 m², parking deeded vs. assigned, quality of the “euro” renovation, utility costs for autonomous heat). Target price range: €200k–€210k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so validate against 2–3 nearby closed/active sales before moving up).

Bottom line

You are buying a high-floor, modern, turnkey family-sized unit with 2 bathrooms and (claimed) underground parking in Centru. You are NOT buying a hyper-buzzy café street (nearby F&B density looks low), and you shouldn’t pay a “prime micro-location” premium unless the exact spot proves it. If the building is well-run and the parking is truly yours on title, it’s a solid long-hold city base.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white variant” finish means you’re buying the shell + engineering, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — duplex layout + 3 bedrooms + multiple bathrooms + terraces fits real day-to-day living. Investor ➖ — 5+ rooms can be harder to rent fast; works only if you target a premium family/expat tenant. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — big terraces + panoramic windows + floor heating = strong “nice to live in” factor.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — compelling size/format (118 m² duplex + terraces) in a quieter Durlești pocket, but you must price in full finishing costs and the realities of a 9/10 floor unit.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €115k–€123k (best-effort; I don’t have reliable comps here). Push on: developer listing, “white variant” finishing budget, and any terrace/waterproofing + elevator/maintenance cost questions. Make the offer conditional on clear documents + technical handover details (heating system, floor-heating zones, windows specs).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern-format duplex with real separation of spaces and two terraces in a calm area with solid everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: move-in convenience or a low-friction rental unit — the finishing phase is where cost overruns and delays usually happen. If the build quality checks out, this is a “pay less now, spend smart later” play — but only at the right entry price.

#150
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · Chișinău, Centru, str. Toma Ciorbă
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely move-in ready on paper (“ready to move” + appliances), but ground-floor + historic house means you must verify moisture/insulation/heating before believing it.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (good centrality; but 1/1 house + ground-floor privacy/noise trade-offs) Investor: ✔ (strong central micro-area; can work as office/salon if legal/HOA allows) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (high ceilings + walkable core; courtyard could be a calm oasis)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great location + 3.40m ceilings are rare, but ground-floor in a historic building is where hidden problems (humidity, paperwork, heating) live.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: (1) proof of legal status/registration and any restrictions for commercial use, (2) recent renovation invoices/photos, (3) heating system details + winter bills, (4) moisture readings on exterior walls, (5) courtyard access/ownership rules. Target price range: €160,000–€175,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). If docs are clean and it’s truly dry/warm in winter, you can justify closer to ask; if any damp/structural/admin quirks appear, push below €165k or walk.

Bottom line

You are buying: central Chișinău walkability with “old-city” proportions (80 sqm + very high ceilings) and potential dual-use (home or work). You are NOT buying: a low-maintenance modern block experience—ground-floor historic stock can mean ongoing capex, comfort quirks, and resale buyer selectivity. If it’s dry, quiet, and legally clean, it’s a character asset; if not, it’s a money pit in a nice postcode.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, autonomous heating + underfloor heating + A/C, gated courtyard.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (130 m², 3 rooms + living, 2 balconies, courtyard/playground nearby) Investor ➖ (premium ticket; ground-floor liquidity depends on light/privacy and resale demand) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (comfortable home-base, but this micro-spot reads more residential/quiet than “walk-out-to-cafés”)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec and size in a newer building, but price is ambitious for Ciocana and floor 1 carries real trade-offs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €295k–€315k (comps unknown, so treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push on: (1) floor 1 discount vs higher floors, (2) limited immediate transit/foot-traffic signal, (3) verify what “garaj” means (deeded garage vs rented/assigned) and whether it’s included. Ask for: proof of building commissioning/acceptance, HOA/maintenance costs, heating system docs, underfloor heating zones, noise/waterproofing on ground floor, and a daylight/privacy check at different times.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, turnkey, newer-build family apartment with comfort tech (autonomous + underfloor heating) and a more controlled, gated setup. You are NOT buying: a café-and-transit-first location or an “always liquid” resale profile—ground floor + Ciocana means you need the price to do some of the work.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (furnished + appliances; “ready to move in” positioning) — but verify renovation quality and any “agency lipstick” in person.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 isolated bedrooms + 2 full bathrooms + level flooring + playground/green courtyard reads like a practical family layout. Investor ➖ — 4-room units can rent, but liquidity is narrower; returns depend heavily on exact condition/building quality and local demand. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — good daily convenience; less of a “city-center cafe crawl” vibe.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/features are compelling, but at 9/10 floor and in a secondary-market building, you need a pricing win and strong building/association checks.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for proof on “no encumbrances,” building maintenance/HOA payments, elevator condition, roof/technical floor issues, and winter heating costs; use any elevator wear, facade/entry deterioration, or noisy exposure (boulevard) to justify a discount. Target price range (best-effort): €160k–€170k (comps not provided; treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a family-forward 4-room with real privacy (isolated bedrooms) + 2 bathrooms + elevator building + decent everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet (boulevard risk) or a “prime central” location; plus the 9th floor is a love-it/hate-it factor. If the building is well-run and the interior is genuinely quality, this can be a very solid long-term live-in pick — just don’t overpay.

(Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

#153
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€127,744 · 3 rooms · Râșcani · str. Calea Moșilor, 4, sectorul Râșcani, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (new-build penthouse with smart-home + autonomous heating), but verify finish quality + waterproofing/terrace details.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, near schools/kindergarten; watch day-to-day amenities) Investor ➖ (premium unit/format; rents well only if finishes + building ops are strong) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (top floor, veranda/terrace vibe, panoramic windows, smart-home)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout and “penthouse/top-floor” appeal, but micro-area looks light on daily walkable life (few food/parks within 300m) and new-build execution risk is real.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €118k–€124k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps for this exact complex, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push hard on: documented total area (99.8 vs 99), terrace/veranda counted how, warranty/hand-over timeline, elevator spec/maintenance fees, noise/roof heat in summer, and any developer “extras” that are still unbuilt.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, new-build 3-room “statement” apartment with modern systems and a bigger-than-average plan. You are NOT buying: a proven, mature neighborhood micro-core right outside your door (expect more reliance on short drives/transit). If the terrace/roof and building management check out, this can feel premium daily; if they don’t, it becomes an expensive headache fast.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€99,900 · 3 rooms · Botanica · strada Independenței 4, Botanica, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances included) — verify wiring/plumbing age and who did the renovation.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (practical 2 bedrooms + living, close to schools/parks) Investor ➖ (easy to rent, but 50 m² / 4-of-5 walk-up caps tenant segment) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (solid day-to-day convenience; less “city-center vibe”)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good “ready to live” package in Botanica, but you’re paying for renovation/furniture and the 4/5 walk-up + small total area needs a discount unless condition is truly top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for documents + utility bills, confirm no damp/mold (top-floor-ish risk is lower here but still check), and push on (1) 4/5 without elevator, (2) compact 50 m² for a “3-room” label, (3) any renovation shortcuts (bathroom waterproofing, electrical panel). Target price range: €90k–€96k (comps not provided, so this is a best-effort discount off list based on typical negotiation levers + ~€2,000/m² implied by the ad). If they can prove recent quality renovation + clean papers, expect them to hold closer to €96k–€99k.

Bottom line

You are buying a compact, efficient 2-bedroom + living that’s already set up (furniture/appliances) with strong everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying space or an “upgrade-proof” building experience — walk-up stairs and 50 m² will be the long-term constraint. If your plan is 5–7 years and you value convenience over size, it’s a sensible play at the right price.
