Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: “cosmetic repair” + partially furnished, but expect finishing/refresh costs to reach a true modern standard.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (104 m², 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies, playground, elevator) Investor ➖ (liquidity ok for a 3-room, but Telecentru + unknown transit/parking realities; rent yield depends heavily on exact building quality) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (some groceries/food nearby, but the immediate 300m seems quieter and transit-light)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout (104 m², 2 baths, 2 balconies) in a new-build tower, but “cosmetic repair” + location convenience signals (transit=0 within 300m) argue for price pressure.

Negotiation strategy

Start by framing it as a “needs-refresh + convenience discount” deal: ask for proof of building quality (developer track record, maintenance fees, utilities, elevator reliability), and push on any wear in parquet/doors, balcony condition, and plumbing in both baths. Target price range: €140k–€147k (best-effort; I don’t have comps for this exact micro-location/building, so treat this as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a big 3-room (104 m²) with the hard-to-find combo of 2 bathrooms + 2 balconies in a newer high-rise. You are NOT buying: a plug-and-play designer renovation or a super “walk-everywhere / transit-at-the-door” lifestyle (based on the limited 300m context). If the building management + utilities check out, this can be a very solid family base on floor 2 (easy stairs as backup).

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 92 m², 5/9 with elevator, practical for daily life in central Chișinău. Investor ➖ — rentable, but price/centrality means yield may be “okay” not stellar; depends on finish + building quality. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — central address, low tourism signal, strong transit density.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (central, 92 m², autonomous heating, elevator), but the pricing needs to reflect building/entry condition, noise, and true 3-room layout quality.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for docs and building basics first (maintenance status, any major repairs planned, heating system service history, elevator upkeep, neighbors/noise). Use “secondary stock + mid-floor” as leverage. Target price range: €138k–€145k (comps not provided, so this is a structure-based guess; if finishes are premium and building is clean/well-run, expect closer to ask).

Bottom line

You are buying central convenience + a generous 92 m² footprint with efficient independent heating. You are NOT buying a quiet suburban bubble — verify street noise, parking reality, and building common-area quality. If the layout truly lives like a modern 3-room (2 beds + living), it’s a keeper; if it’s a compromised “2+living” marketed as 3, push harder on price.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances, autonomous heat; typical “pack your bags” unit).

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 2 separate bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + parks/schools vibe nearby. Investor ➖ — good “new-build, furnished” rentability, but price is premium for Ciocana; yield depends on exact micro-location/building fees. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — 7th floor, balconies, elevator, walkable daily needs; less “old-town charm,” more practical comfort.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and building package, but you should push on price and verify building/floor data consistency before paying top-of-market.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €185,000–€199,000 (best-effort estimate; I don’t have verified comps for this exact Braus complex and finish level). Use: (1) data inconsistency (7/19 mentioned vs “17 floors” elsewhere), (2) ask for proof of autonomous heating costs/maintenance, (3) elevator/HOA fees, (4) furniture/appliance inventory & warranties, (5) any mortgage-eligibility constraints (Prima Casă) to frame a “clean buyer, fast close” discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, family-friendly 3-room with real usability (wardrobe, 2 baths, 2 balconies) in a practical, well-served pocket of Ciocana. You are NOT buying: a bargain or a historic/quiet boutique block—this is a big-complex, main-boulevard lifestyle where noise/traffic and HOA rules can matter. Check: actual total floors + exact floor, view/noise exposure to bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, and building management reputation.

€139,900 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · Chișinău, Telecentru, Str. Grenoble
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — it’s a “grey/white” finish penthouse shell, so budget time + money before living in it.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (space + 2 bathrooms, but only if you’re ready for a build-out) Investor ➖ (unique unit helps, but liquidity depends on finishing quality + true comp set) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (top floor, 3m ceilings, panoramic glazing, potential terrace)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the unit can be a gem (top floor, new brick building, big area), but you’re buying a construction project and you should be paid (via price) for that risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted offer for an unfinished top-floor unit). Push on: (1) unfinished condition (full fit-out cost), (2) 10/10 floor trade-offs (heat/wind/elevator dependence), (3) verify terrace legality/area if mentioned, (4) confirm what “variant” includes (screed, wiring, heating boiler/radiators, plumbing stubs, window spec). Ask for a full cost-to-finish estimate and make the offer contingent on document + systems checks.

Bottom line

You are buying: volume + light + flexibility in a newer brick building (the expensive-to-recreate fundamentals). You are NOT buying: a turnkey home — expect a real renovation timeline, contractor management, and a finishing budget that can swing wildly. If the view/ceiling height/plan potential matter to you, negotiate hard and then finish it properly once, not cheaply twice.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Школы, детские сады, медицинские учреждения – все в шаговой доступности”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 4 grocery shops, 1 food/drink places, 76 parks/playgrounds, and 7 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Telecentru.

Street (from listing fields): Chișinău.

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

#159
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€92,700 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Zelinschi, 26/2, sec. Botanica, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: cosmetic renovation + furnished + double-glazed windows suggests you can live there immediately, but expect some refresh spend to make it feel “new”.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms, nearby school/kindergarten, parks, mid-floor walk-up feels practical. Investor ➖ — good rental fundamentals (transit + groceries + amenities), but 4/5 without confirmed elevator can cap demand/price ceiling. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — decent day-to-day convenience; less “premium” vibe implied by secondary stock + cosmetic repairs.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid Botanica micro-location and a very standard, liquid 3-room layout (60 m²), but it’s not a standout product; let price/terms do the work.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for documentation + clarity first (building year/series, heating type, HOA/maintenance, any major planned works). Use “cosmetic repair” and likely no-elevator walk-up as leverage. Target price range (best-effort, comps unknown): €85,000–€90,000. If condition is truly clean and paperwork is perfect, expect the seller to defend closer to €90k+.

Bottom line

You are buying a practical, liveable 3-room in a convenience-heavy pocket (parks + groceries + transit). You are NOT buying a premium renovated finish or a building with obvious “status” features. If stairs/elevator and building upkeep check out, it’s a sensible, low-drama buy at the right number.

#160
Apartament cu 4 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 4 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€135,000 · 4 rooms · Râșcani · Chișinău, sect. Râșcani, bd. Moscova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances), but verify workmanship and building systems given the high floor.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 4 rooms + 2 bathrooms, parks/schools nearby, playground and elevator. Investor ➖ — strong rentability for large families/roommates, but unit size/maintenance and resale liquidity depend heavily on layout and building quality. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — good daily convenience (cafes/groceries/transit), but it’s more “practical city living” than charming/quiet.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good micro-location signals and functional spec, but price needs to reflect panel-block risk, high-floor realities, and unknown true condition.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €118k–€128k (comps unknown; this range is a risk-adjusted starting point). Push on: (1) high floor (13/15) + elevator reliability, (2) renovation age/quality proof (receipts, photos pre/post), (3) any moisture/ventilation issues, (4) parking being “open” not guaranteed, (5) balcony listed as “No” (some buyers discount that hard). Ask for: latest utility bills, association fees, and any planned building works.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, functional Râșcani family apartment with strong everyday amenities density and transit access. You are NOT buying: a low-risk building asset without due diligence—high-floor + secondary stock means you must audit elevators, plumbing, and renovation quality. You’re paying for “ready now” convenience; make sure the renovation isn’t just cosmetic.

#161
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€140,000 · 3 rooms · Poșta Veche · str. Calea Orheiului, 69
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: recent renovation, sold furnished, kitchen equipped, elevator building.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 72 m², 2 bathrooms, functional layout) Investor ➖ (rentable “ready-to-go”, but Poșta Veche + price level needs yield check) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (practical, not a café/nightlife micro-area; more “get-things-done” urban living)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid turnkey family apartment in a convenient, transit-heavy pocket, but you should pressure-test the price vs. block type/area vibe and verify renovation quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (best-effort; true comps not provided). Use: panel-building stock (series 143), floor 5/9, Poșta Veche micro-area calmness vs. limited food/drink density nearby, and “developer/agent” pricing posture. Ask for: proof of renovation scope (wiring/plumbing), exact total vs. living area split, heating bills, and elevator/building maintenance status. If anything is “cosmetic eurorepair only,” anchor closer to the low end.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-friendly, turnkey 3-room with 2 bathrooms and good daily-life infrastructure + transit. You are NOT buying: a prime central vibe or a dense walk-to-dinner neighborhood; this is convenience-first, not lifestyle-first. Pressure-test: renovation quality + building health (elevator, common areas, utilities) — that’s where panel-block deals go from great to annoying.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€125,900 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Cetatea Albă 3, Botanica, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — “individual design”, furnished, autonomous heat, AC, elevator; low hassle if the finish quality checks out.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground, parks nearby) Investor ➖ (rentable layout, but price vs bank valuation gap may cap yield) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (quiet-enough Botanica pocket with daily amenities; not touristy)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional package (86.4 m², floor 2/9, 2 apts per landing, autonomous heating), but the stated bank valuation (~€115k) weakens the €125.9k ask.

Negotiation strategy

Start from the financing reality: if the bank values it ~€115k, many buyers get constrained on leverage. Use that as your anchor and push for seller-side flexibility. Target price range: €112,000–€118,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a disciplined financing-based range, not a market comp claim). Ask for: proof of autonomous heating documentation/servicing, building maintenance fee history, elevator/common-area condition, and a clause covering any unpermitted remodel elements.

Bottom line

You are buying: a larger 3-room + living in a mid-rise block with convenient daily life, low-tourism vibe, and a “second floor” sweet spot. You are NOT buying: a prime central nightlife/walk-everywhere district; this is more practical Botanica than “showpiece downtown”. Watch-outs: verify daylight/noise (street-facing?), finish quality behind the “design” label, and parking reality in the micro-area.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated + furnished), but verify renovation quality and building systems typical for panel stock.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 rooms + 2 bathrooms + 3 balconies is rare utility, good day-to-day layout potential. Investor ➖ — rentable, but 127k pricing only works if condition/building checks out and rent is strong for Traian/Botanica. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — good “liveable city” micro-area: groceries + food + transit density without tourist noise.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (72 m², 2 baths, balconies, mid-floor, AC, elevator) but “panel/series” + agency-led listing means you should pressure-test price and renovation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €115,000–€122,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Ask for: proof of 72 m² in documents, renovation year/photos of wiring/plumbing, heating bill history, elevator/roof/capex plans, and clarify what “separate entrance” actually means (true private entry vs stairwell positioning). Use panel-building concerns + agency boilerplate + any wear in finishes to justify a step-down.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-friendly 3-room with unusually good “utility” (2 bathrooms, 3 balconies) in a transit-connected Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: a premium new-build experience — panel stock can mean noise/thermal quirks, and “Euro-renovation” varies wildly. If documents + building condition check out, this is a convenience play; if not, it becomes an expensive cosmetics trap.

On-the-ground checks

  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

€139,900 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Grenoble, sect. Botanica
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, appliances included — but double-check the heating type (block-level vs “autonomous” wording conflict).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (2 separate bedrooms + living, nearby schools/kindergartens, gated courtyard/playground) Investor: ➖ (good “ready to rent” package, but 15/16 floor + airport-facing view narrows tenant pool) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (high-floor view, on-site services, modern complex with underground parking)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout + modern complex + turnkey condition, but price per m² likely ambitious for Botanica unless the finish/building quality and parking situation are genuinely top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (best-effort guess; no direct comps provided). Push on (1) high-floor risk (wind/heat, elevator dependence), (2) airport-side noise/air quality sensitivity, (3) clarify what “heating” really is and monthly costs, (4) confirm underground parking terms (included/optional/cost). Offer fast closing contingent on documents + building management fee proof.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern 60 m², 2-bed + living, high-floor “turnkey” unit in a closed-courtyard complex with commerce/services downstairs. You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-floor, easy-stairs backup home — this is elevator-lifestyle and location/exposure matters. If the view is your thing, great; if you’re noise-sensitive, test it at peak hours before falling in love.

€220,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Melestiu, sec. Botanica
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“Euroreparație”), furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, park access, playground mentioned) Investor: ➖ (good liquidity/location, but premium price—yields depend heavily on exact rent) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (next to Valea Trandafirilor + across from MallDova; easy daily life)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location + turnkey spec, but price needs to be justified by building quality, view/noise, and true condition.

Negotiation strategy

Start by verifying the basics that drive value here: exact building class/developer, legal docs, HOA/maintenance, elevator count, insulation quality ("interior insulation" can be a moisture risk), and noise levels facing the mall/arterials. If everything checks out, still negotiate on “turnkey premium”. Target price range: €200,000–€210,000 (comps not provided, so this is a best-effort range based on a negotiation discount from the ask for a 93 m², new-build, renovated unit in a prime Botanica pocket). If any red flags (humidity/ventilation, cheap finishes, noisy exposure), push €185,000–€200,000.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern-feeling 93 m² layout in a very convenient Botanica micro-area with park + mall right there. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/air quality—mall-facing views often mean traffic noise and dust; confirm before paying a premium. This is a “pay for convenience” apartment: worth it if you value park walks + urban access more than absolute calm.

Investor ➖ — rentable, but panel block + 8th floor means tenant pool is price-sensitive; returns depend heavily on actual interior quality. Lifestyle buyer ➖ — practical daily convenience and transit are decent, but it’s more functional residential than “cute walk-everywhere” urban.

Final verdict

You are NOT buying: a premium building or a designer renovation — it’s a sensible panel-block deal where the numbers must be right. If the interior is clean and systems (plumbing/electrics/elevator) check out, this is a solid “live now, upgrade later” play.

Needs work: “white variant” finish means you’re buying a new-build shell + systems, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (87 m², 3 rooms, playground + quieter edge-of-city setting) Investor ➖ (developer-rate plan is a plus, but micro-area amenities look thin; resale/rent demand will be more price-sensitive) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (good for calm + views, less good for walk-to-everything city energy)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good new-build package (underground parking, elevator, autonomous heating) but you should price in finishing costs and Durlești liquidity.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for an all-in comparison: base price + any mandatory “extras” + parking terms + finishing timeline. Push for a discount or included items (e.g., partial finishing, kitchen/doors, or parking). Target price range: €88,000–€93,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range based on finish-stage + location + new-build marketing premium).

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern new-build 3-room layout with a developer story (views, underground parking, premium elevator). You are NOT buying: a highly walkable, cafe-dense central neighborhood—this is more “drive/commute and chill.”

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€173,250 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Str. Gradina Botanica, 18, Chișinău, Botanica
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build “white box” finish + still in delivery phase means you’re buying a timeline, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 105 m², 2 bathrooms, practical layout, big green space across the street is a real quality-of-life multiplier. Investor ➖ — rentable once delivered, but finish-out capex + delivery risk + pricing needs to be sharp. Lifestyle buyer ✔ — park-adjacent, calmer micro-area, good “daily walk” energy without being touristy.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive size/location next to the Botanical Garden, but the value hinges on delivery certainty and total all-in cost (finishing + fees + parking terms).

Negotiation strategy

Ask for written clarity on handover date, what “white variant” includes, and parking pricing/ownership; push for price based on delivery risk. Target price range (best-effort): €160k–€170k (comps not provided, so this is risk-adjusted, not comp-anchored). If they won’t move, negotiate upgrades/finish credits or a stronger delivery/penalty clause equivalent in value.

Bottom line

You are buying space (105 m²) and a strong green-edge location in a modern building. You are NOT buying certainty on move-in date or final cost until the finish spec + handover terms are nailed down. If the paperwork is clean and delivery is real, this is a high-comfort family setup; if not, it’s an expensive construction bet.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms in Centru + park proximity is a strong everyday-life combo, if noise is manageable) Investor ➖ (rentability likely decent in Centru, but 56 m² limits “premium family” positioning; depends on layout/condition) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (central-ish convenience + lots of transit; good for car-light living)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — location and floor (3/5) look solid, but the ad is thin on the two things that move price most: condition and building quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €80,000–€86,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps from this specific micro-area/building). Iuri Gagarin.

Bottom line

You are buying central convenience + park access with strong transit connectivity. You are NOT buying certainty: the ad gives almost nothing about renovation quality, utilities, or building upkeep. If the layout is efficient and the building is clean/quiet, this can be a very livable “daily driver” apartment — otherwise it’s an overpay at ask.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good Centru positioning and 3-room layout, but quality/condition is ambiguous; don’t pay “renovated” pricing without proof.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82k–€90k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps for this exact building/condition). Bottom line

You are buying a central, high-floor 3-room unit with 2 bathrooms and elevator access in a busy, practical corridor. You are NOT buying guaranteed move-in-ready finish quality unless the seller can reconcile the “furnished” claim with the “no renovation” condition. Expect trade-offs: traffic/noise, wind/heat on a high floor, and likely refresh costs.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper: renovated (“Euroreparație”), furnished, with appliances, elevator/intercom; verify workmanship + wiring/plumbing.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ — 3 bedrooms + 2 bathrooms + playground and schools nearby is the pitch; 8/9th floor elevator life is fine if it’s reliable. Investor ➖ — rentable 3-room, but the Muncești corridor can be noisier; resale/rent depends heavily on exact micro-spot + building condition. Lifestyle buyer ❌ — the immediate 300m amenity signal looks thin (few groceries, no parks/food/drink counted), so it’s more “practical base” than “walk-out-to-cafés” living.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional layout (2 baths, 2 balconies, storage) and renovation helps, but price/value hinges on confirmed 74 m² vs the obvious “674 mp” typo, plus building/area noise and true walkability.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €98k–€105k (comps unknown; range is a risk-adjusted ask for 74 m², 8/9 floor, renovated, secondary stock). Push on: (1) renovation age/receipts, (2) elevator + roof/top-floor-adjacent risks (even if you’re not top floor), (3) sound insulation/road noise, (4) building maintenance fund and any upcoming capital repairs. Ask for a daytime + late-evening viewing on a weekday.

Bottom line

You are buying: a “get-things-done” 3-room in Botanica with space efficiency (2 baths, balconies) and a ready-to-live setup. You are NOT buying: a prime walkable café/park micro-neighbourhood right outside the door (based on the limited 300m POI signal). If the building is well-run and the noise is acceptable, it’s a good family home at the right price — not a trophy location deal.

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the product is compelling (169 m² penthouse, terrace, premium building spec), but you must price in off-plan/hand-over risk and the full cost of finishing.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a developer-style package: price concession OR finish/upgrade credits (HVAC, better glazing spec confirmation, terrace waterproofing warranty terms, elevator access to top floor, parking inclusion). Best-effort target: €270k–€295k (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range rather than a comp-driven “true value”). Push hardest if parking is not included or if handover terms are soft.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, modern-shell penthouse with real “wow” features (terrace, panoramic windows, high ceilings) in a quieter Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in certainty or a central, high-liquid resale product — your exit depends on execution and handover. You’re also buying the micro-area vibe: lots of nearby green space signals, decent daily groceries, and workable transit access — more practical than flashy.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 3 food/drink places, 99 parks/playgrounds, and 11 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

#177
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€92,500 · 3 rooms · Botanica · bd. Traian, 4, Botanica, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — sounds structurally “fine” but top-floor + “first line” boulevard exposure means you must verify roof, noise, and windows before calling it plug‑and‑play.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, transit + groceries nearby; balconies add day-to-day livability) Investor ➖ (rentable, but boulevard noise + 10/10 liquidity depends heavily on building/roof condition) Lifestyle buyer ➖ (great convenience; less “quiet-chic” because it’s on a main artery)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong Botanica positioning and 72 m² is solid, but 10/10 + boulevard frontage is where expensive surprises (roof/heat/noise) hide.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82,000–€89,000 (comps not provided; this range assumes you’ll price in top-floor/roof risk and boulevard noise unless documentation/renovation quality clearly offsets it). Use a checklist-driven negotiation: 1) roof inspection / last overhaul proof, 2) heating + utility bills, 3) window quality/noise at peak hours, 4) elevator reliability (if any), 5) legal clarity (seller is “developer” + secondary stock).

Bottom line

You are buying convenience in central Botanica: groceries + transit density and a functional family layout with 2 bathrooms. You are NOT buying serenity — “first line” on bd. Traian and a top floor can be amazing or annoying, depending on roof, glazing, and building maintenance. If the building is well-run and the view is real, it can feel premium; if not, it becomes a constant small tax on comfort.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, appliances included; expect only taste-level tweaks.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (big 175 m², 2 bathrooms, parking, parks nearby) Investor ➖ (high ticket; rental yield likely secondary to capital preservation) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (Centru + “club house” vibe, autonomy, privacy)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong specs (size + parking + finished) but the price is where deals are won/lost.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor off €/m²: at 325k for 175 m² you’re paying ~€1,860/m² (before any “club house” premium). Without verified comps for this micro-area/building, assume negotiation is possible: target €295,000–€315,000 depending on actual building quality, soundproofing, and whether parking is deeded/guaranteed. Use (1) age/quality of building systems, (2) elevator/maintenance fees, (3) sunlight + noise on Ciuflea, and (4) furniture/appliance value realism as leverage.

Bottom line

You are buying space and convenience in Centru with a finished interior and parking. You are NOT buying a “cheap per m²” deal; you’re paying for turnkey + low-friction living. If the building is truly quiet and well-managed, it’s a keeper; if not, the street exposure becomes the tax.

Move-in readiness

Depends: “white variant” shell finish — core systems are there, but expect meaningful fit-out cost/time before comfortable living.

Buyer fit

Family ✔ (huge layout, 3 beds + living, 2 baths, terrace; schools/parks nearby) Investor ➖ (premium ticket size + atypical 174 m²; great if finished well, slower resale/rent pool) Lifestyle buyer ✔ (top-floor terrace + privacy; good if you like space and can handle finishing)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — excellent volume and top-floor perks, but “unfinished” risk + Telecentru location means price must compensate.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on finishing uncertainty (white-box), top-floor/roof risk (heat, waterproofing, noise from lift machinery), and liquidity of very large units. Ask for proof of documents/acceptance, utilities, and elevator status to 12th. Target price range: €195k–€210k (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted buyer-friendly range; go higher only if finish budget is already minimal and the building quality is clearly strong).

Bottom line

You are buying: a “penthouse-like” amount of space (174 m²) with terrace + 2 baths in a new-build, plus parks and basic daily convenience close by. You are NOT buying: instant turnkey living, nor a super-liquid standard 3-room unit; you’re also taking on top-floor building-quality risk unless verified. You should pay: only if the finishing budget + timeline still makes the all-in cost look like a deal versus finished alternatives.
